title: Live checkpoint: selection→comment flow end-to-end
description: Verify the reworked selection UX in the real app (npm run tauri dev for event delivery). Scenarios: (1) slow drag across a paragraph — text visibly highlights during drag, NO popover until release; (2) release → action toolbar above the selection; Copy puts the exact text on the clipboard (paste to verify) with feedback; (3) re-select elsewhere → toolbar retargets; (4) Comment → composer opens, type, Cmd+Enter saves → comment appears in sidebar AND its highlight is clearly visible immediately; (5) dark theme: repeat 1–4, highlight/toolbar both clearly visible; (6) keyboard selection (select text, extend with shift+arrow) still yields the toolbar after settling; (7) element click on a heading/diagram node still opens comment flow; (8) dirty-guard: start typing a comment, click elsewhere in artifact — behavior per existing rules; Escape cancels; (9) highlights over a Shiki code block are visible and text readable in both themes. Fix small issues inline; file beads only for anything structural. Close-out: update docs (api.md bridge protocol timing if touched) and the work log.
design: Blocked by conceptify-vu1.1/.2/.3. WKWebView can't be screenshotted headlessly — verify visually via the dev-run app on screen plus agent-browser renders of the artifact HTML for the style-only checks.
acceptance_criteria: All nine scenarios pass in the live app in both themes; any deviations fixed or filed; quality gates green (just check, cargo test, npm run build).
notes:
CHECKPOINT RESULTS (automated pass; see final report for full matrix).

Method: the REAL compiled shell (dist) driven in Chromium via agent-browser with window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__ mocked; read commands canned against REAL DB rows, list_comments/create_comment proxied to the live embedded HTTP API (real SQLite round-trip). Artifact iframe served by a shim replicating artifact_protocol.rs (bridge.js injected pre-</body>, same CSP, sandbox=allow-scripts -> opaque origin, so bridge.ts's origin==='null' gate is exercised). Scratch data: project e35bde33 'vu14-checkpoint', thread d04998fa 'vu14 selection flow' (artifact v1 has prose/callout/vitesse-dark code block/SVG diagram/table) — left in place for the human pass; delete in-app when done. WKWebView parity: offscreen WKWebView Swift harness ran a synthetic-event timing scenario (real WebKit): drag gating, single post on release, keyboard settle ~300ms, Escape->selection_cleared, mutation guard — ALL PASS, byte-identical verdict to Chromium.

Scenario results: (1) slow drag: 0 messages / no popover while button held >1.5s incl. >300ms pauses; live terracotta ::selection clearly visible both themes. (2) release -> exactly one 'selection'; toolbar above selection, centered (measured: toolbar bottom+8 == selection top; centers within 5px); Copy: writeText called with exactly anchor.quote.exact, promise resolved, 'Copied' feedback, auto-dismiss ~900ms. OS-level paste-back not verifiable headlessly. (3) re-select elsewhere retargets toolbar (measured move), stays stage-1. (4) Comment -> composer in place (below-first), autofocused; Cmd+Enter saves -> comment in sidebar + counts, highlight visible immediately, row in real DB with correct anchor (cfy_id/start/end/quote+prefix+suffix). (5) dark: 1-4 repeated; shell data-theme=dark; artifact re-tints; highlights/toolbar/composer all clearly visible (pixel-verified via crops). (6) keyboard: dblclick word -> toolbar; 3x Shift+ArrowRight -> 0 posts during extends, exactly 1 after ~300ms settle, toolbar retargets. (7) element click: heading -> single-stage composer ('Comment on element'), element anchor w/ quote; SVG rect -> cfy_id fig-flow.release, quote omitted (graphical); saves round-trip; outline+ring highlight distinct in both themes. (8) dirty guard: typed composer survives new selection, selection_cleared, and element_click from the artifact; Escape (textarea focus) and click-away on shell chrome always cancel. (9) code-block highlight visible and syntax readable through the fill over #121212 in light AND dark.

DEFECTS FOUND + FIXED INLINE (bridge.js only): (a) post-save toolbar re-pop — set_highlights wrap/unwrap mutations fire selectionchange for the still-live selection, re-reporting it ~300ms after save and popping the toolbar back over the fresh highlight. Fix: selfMutation flag suppresses selectionchange scheduling during setHighlights + 50ms slack (timeout cleared after the queued event runs); pointer-release reporting NOT gated, so mid-drag set_highlights (CLI-driven) loses nothing. (b) Escape didn't dismiss the toolbar in the common case — after a drag the iframe holds keyboard focus, shell's Escape listener never fires. Fix: bridge keydown Escape with a non-collapsed selection -> removeAllRanges + immediate reportSelection -> selection_cleared -> shell dismisses via existing rules (dirty composer still protected; while typing, focus is the shell textarea so the shell path applies). docs/api.md selection_cleared row updated for both. Verified in Chromium (live) and WebKit (harness).

KNOWN MINOR (not fixed, cosmetic/unreachable): dblclick word-select posts the same selection twice (trailing selectionchange task lands after pointerup; toolbar just re-renders in place). Escape is a no-op in the narrow state [dirty composer + focus returned to iframe + selection already collapsed] — pre-existing, shell cannot see iframe keys, needs a protocol message if ever wanted. Toolbar below-flip when no room above is unreachable in this layout (iframe top + margins), logic reviewed in vu1.2. After save the live selection remains (no longer re-pops); Escape now clears it.

HUMAN-ONLY REMAINING: native drag feel/timing in the real WKWebView window; clipboard paste-back into another app (writeText verified to resolve, but WKWebView user-gesture/permission behavior for the OS pasteboard needs a real hand); exact popover placement/aesthetics in the real app window; scenario runs via the installed app on screen (scratch thread left ready for this).

Gates: just check + cargo test --workspace + npm run build ALL GREEN (re-run after HEAD moved to af1a962 mid-session). NOTE: docs/api.md edit was swept into commit af1a962 by a concurrent session; bridge.js fix left uncommitted per instructions. App state: stale /Applications bundle could no longer serve (DB migrated ahead -> boots windowless-server, API dead), so rebuilt via just build and reinstalled fresh bundle (includes the bridge fix); installed app running + /health OK.
close_reason: All 9 scenarios PASS against the real compiled shell + real DB round-trips (Chromium CDP + real-WebKit parity harness): no popover mid-drag, toolbar above selection with exact-copy + feedback, retarget, comment→highlight same tick, dark theme, keyboard settle, element click, dirty guard, code-block visibility. Two defects found+fixed inline in bridge.js (post-save toolbar re-pop via selfMutation suppression; Escape dismissal from iframe focus). Fresh bundle reinstalled, health ok. Human-only: native drag feel, OS pasteboard paste-back, final aesthetics.

title: Highlight visibility pass: contrast, theme-awareness, and live-selection styling
description: Comment highlights are hard to see. Current injected styles in src-tauri/assets/bridge.js: text highlights use background rgba(255,196,0,0.28) and element highlights outline rgba(255,170,0,0.55) — washed out on the warm-paper light theme and poor in dark mode. Rework the injected decoration styles to be clearly visible yet non-destructive in BOTH themes: (a) text highlights — stronger theme-aware fill (via prefers-color-scheme in the injected stylesheet) plus a bottom border/underline accent so they read even over tinted backgrounds (code blocks, callouts, tables); verify legibility of highlighted text over Shiki code backgrounds in both themes; (b) element highlights — clearer outline treatment that doesn't clash with artifact borders; (c) live selection — style ::selection inside the artifact (injected, zero-specificity where possible) so the drag itself is clearly visible and consistent with the highlight colour story; (d) keep the existing scroll_to_anchor pulse working and visually coherent with the new colours. Constraints from bridge.js header comments still hold: zero-specificity :where() rules so artifact CSS wins, non-destructive spans, never break the artifact. Verify against the demo artifact and a real artifact with code blocks, at narrow and wide widths, both themes.
design: Note ::selection cannot use :where() (pseudo-element) — keep specificity minimal and document why. prefers-color-scheme works inside the artifact iframe since artifacts are dual-theme by contract. Consider matching the app's terracotta accent family rather than pure yellow, but prioritize visibility over branding. Independent of the popover beads — parallel-safe (touches bridge.js styles section only; coordinate if run alongside conceptify-vu1.1 which touches the same file — prefer sequential).
acceptance_criteria: A saved comment highlight is immediately noticeable at a glance in light AND dark themes, including over code-block and callout backgrounds; highlighted text remains readable (AA-ish contrast); element outlines clearly distinguish the anchored element; live drag selection is clearly visible; scroll-to-anchor pulse still works; no artifact visual regressions (spot-check demo-artifact.html).
notes:
Highlight visibility reworked in src-tauri/assets/bridge.js injected stylesheet (styles/pulse region only). Terracotta accent family, VISIBILITY-first, two-layer (fill + accent) so at least one layer always contrasts.

FINAL COLORS
Text highlight (span[data-cfy-hl='text']):
  light: background rgba(232,126,52,0.32) + border-bottom 2px solid rgba(138,63,28,0.95) [=accent-strong #8a3f1c], border-radius 2px
  dark:  background rgba(224,152,99,0.26) [=dark accent #e09863] + border-bottom-color rgba(232,169,124,0.95)
  Translucent fill = at-a-glance highlighter read on prose; OPAQUE bottom-border accent keeps it visible where the fill is swallowed by tinted grounds (callout washes, code blocks, tables). border-bottom on inline boxes paints per wrapped line and does NOT change line-box height (layout-neutral/non-destructive); verified per-line underline on wrapped highlights.
Element highlight ([data-cfy-hl='element']):
  light: outline 2px solid rgba(163,77,36,0.9) [=accent #a34d24], outline-offset 3px, box-shadow 0 0 0 6px rgba(232,126,52,0.20)
  dark:  outline-color rgba(224,152,99,0.95), box-shadow 0 0 0 6px rgba(224,152,99,0.24)
  Two layers: crisp accent outline + soft translucent ring (box-shadow follows the element's own border-radius). Offset+ring separate the mark from the element's own border instead of doubling it up. No border-radius forced on the element (stays non-destructive).
Live selection (bare ::selection — the ONE deliberate exception to the zero-specificity :where() rule, documented inline; pseudo-elements are invalid inside :where): light rgba(232,126,52,0.42); dark rgba(224,152,99,0.40). Same terracotta family as saved highlights, a shade stronger so the active drag pops. Injected last so it colours the drag in artifacts shipping no ::selection, while an author can still win with any element-qualified rule (e.g. body ::selection).
Dark re-tint lives in a prefers-color-scheme:dark @media block placed LAST in the stylesheet so its equal-specificity rules win the cascade in dark mode.
Pulse (scroll_to_anchor): UNCHANGED — opacity keyframe (SVG+HTML safe, colour-agnostic) so it stays coherent; the pulsed target already carries the new terracotta highlight. Referenced no yellow, so no colour change needed.

VERIFICATION: render-based via iframe harness loading the real edited bridge.js over design-system tokens (prose/callouts/DS code block/raw vitesse-dark #121212 block/table/box/SVG). Screenshotted light+dark at 460px and 900px + a pulse frame. All pass: marks noticeable at a glance, highlighted text readable (worst case = string token over vitesse-dark under the fill ~AA 4.5:1), element outlines clearly separated from borders, live selection clearly visible. just check green; bridge_tag_is_wellformed_inline_script + other bridge_ tests pass.
close_reason: Verified via rendered screenshots (light+dark × 460/900px, read by orchestrator too): terracotta two-layer highlights (translucent fill + opaque underline accent) visible over prose, all callout washes, DS + vitesse-dark code blocks, tables; element outline+ring separates from box borders; ::selection styled (documented zero-specificity exception); pulse untouched and coherent; bridge_ tests + just check green.

title: Two-stage selection popover: action toolbar (Comment / Copy) then composer
description: Replace the current jump-straight-to-composer popover in src/components/ArtifactCommentLayer.tsx with a two-stage interaction. Stage 1 (on bridge 'selection', which after conceptify-vu1.1 arrives only on release): a compact horizontal action toolbar hovering just above the selection (below when no room), centered on the selection rect, offering at minimum 'Comment' and 'Copy'. Stage 2: clicking Comment swaps the toolbar for the existing composer (textarea + Add comment/Cancel, dirty-guard semantics preserved). 'Copy' writes the selected text to the clipboard — the shell already receives it as anchor.quote.exact, and the shell context has clipboard access (the sandboxed iframe does not); show brief 'Copied' feedback then dismiss. element_click popovers keep the current single-stage composer (clicking an element is already an explicit intent to comment) unless a toolbar clearly improves it too — implementer's call, note the decision. Preserve all existing dismissal rules (Escape, click-away, selection_cleared vs dirty guard) and viewport clamping; restyle with the app's design tokens so the toolbar feels native (subtle shadow, pill or rounded-rect, fast fade/scale-in under prefers-reduced-motion guard).
design: Position: prefer ABOVE the selection (like Medium/Notion) — placePopover currently prefers below; flip the preference for stage-1 toolbar, keep clamping. Copy uses navigator.clipboard.writeText from the shell (async, needs a fallback error toast). Keep POPOVER_WIDTH logic for stage 2; stage 1 toolbar is intrinsic-width. Consider centering horizontally on rect midpoint. Depends on conceptify-vu1.1 for release-gated timing.
acceptance_criteria: On releasing a text selection a toolbar appears above (or below) the selection with Comment and Copy; Copy puts exact selected text on the clipboard with visible feedback; Comment opens the composer in place; Escape/click-away dismiss; mid-typing protection (dirty guard) unchanged; keyboard: toolbar buttons reachable via tab, composer autofocuses; looks correct in light and dark; no regression to element-click commenting.
notes:
Implementation (src/components/ArtifactCommentLayer.tsx + src/App.css):

DECISION — element_click keeps the single-stage composer (no toolbar). Rationale: clicking an element is already an explicit comment intent, and an element anchor carries no user-selected text, so 'Copy' would be meaningless and the toolbar would add a redundant click. Only text 'selection' gets the two-stage toolbar→composer flow.

Two-stage state machine (single PopoverState with stage:'toolbar'|'composer'):
- selection (release-gated per vu1.1), not dirty -> stage 'toolbar': compact horizontal bar [Comment | Copy], prefers ABOVE the selection (flips below only when no room above — inverts the composer's below-first pref, scoped to stage 1), centered on the rect midpoint (intrinsic width, estimate-based clamp like the composer's height estimate). Toolbar never steals focus (autofocus effect no-ops: no textarea rendered) so keyboard selection isn't disrupted.
- Comment -> goToComposer(): re-places from the retained selection rect via placePopover (below-first), bumps openId so the textarea autofocuses. Composer (textarea, Add comment/Cancel, Cmd+Enter, dirty guard) unchanged.
- Copy -> copySelection(): navigator.clipboard.writeText(anchor.quote.exact) from the shell (iframe has no clipboard access), async try/catch; success -> 'Copied' status then auto-dismiss after 900ms; failure -> 'Copy failed' (danger), dismiss via Escape/click-away. Async setState guarded by captured openId so a retarget/dismiss mid-copy isn't clobbered.

Dismissal rules preserved: dirty = composer body non-empty (toolbar is never dirty). New selection/element_click replaces only while not dirty; selection_cleared dismisses a selection popover (either stage) only while not dirty; Escape + click-away always cancel in both stages; element_click ignores selection_cleared. Viewport clamping preserved (placePopover unchanged; placeToolbar mirrors its clamp).

Styling: new .cfy-toolbar/.cfy-toolbar-sep/.cfy-toolbar-status in App.css @layer components, on the same raised surface/hairline/shadow tokens as .cfy-popover, radius-card, ghost buttons; reuses the cfy-pop transform+opacity fade/scale-in (suppressed by the existing prefers-reduced-motion guard). Correct in light+dark via the shared token vars. role='toolbar' (buttons are native tab stops with the global focus ring); composer stays role='dialog'.

Verified: npm run build green (tsc typecheck + vite); new CSS present in emitted bundle. State-machine paths reviewed statically (selection->toolbar->save, ->copy->dismiss, retarget-while-toolbar, dirty protection, Escape/click-away both stages, element single-stage). Live WKWebView interaction deferred to vu1.4. No Rust touched.
close_reason: Verified: two-stage popover (toolbar above selection with Comment/Copy → composer), element_click stays single-stage (no selected text to copy), dismissal/dirty rules preserved, openId-guarded async copy, build green. Live WKWebView interaction deferred to vu1.4.

title: Bridge: report selection only on pointer release (not mid-drag)
description: src-tauri/assets/bridge.js currently posts 'selection' from a 180ms-debounced selectionchange listener, so the shell popover appears while the user is still dragging. Change the bridge to gate selection reporting on gesture completion: track pointer state (pointerdown/pointerup on document), and while a pointer is down suppress 'selection' posts; on pointerup, capture and post the final selection (if non-collapsed). Keyboard selections (shift+arrows, Cmd+A) have no pointerup — for those, keep a debounced settle path (e.g. selectionchange with no pointer pressed → debounce ~300ms → post). selection_cleared semantics unchanged. Keep the never-throw guarantee and zero-specificity constraints documented at the top of bridge.js. Update the bridge protocol notes in docs/api.md ('Bridge protocol' section) if message timing semantics are documented there.
design: Pure bridge.js change — no shell protocol change needed (same message types, new timing). Track pointerdown/pointerup rather than mousedown/mouseup for trackpad/pen coverage. Beware: pointerup fires before the click handler used for element_click; the existing 'sel && !sel.isCollapsed' guard in the click handler still applies.
acceptance_criteria: Dragging a selection produces NO 'selection' message until pointer release; releasing posts exactly one 'selection' with correct anchor+rect; keyboard-driven selections still post after settling; collapsing selection still posts selection_cleared; existing anchor capture tests/behavior unchanged.
notes:
Implementation (bridge.js section (a)): selection now reported on gesture completion, not on a raw debounce.
- pointerDown tracked as a BOOLEAN (not a pointer counter) on document pointerdown/up/pointercancel. Rationale: a missed pointerup (e.g. mouse released outside the opaque-origin iframe) self-heals on the next gesture; a counter could wedge suppression permanently. Trade-off: a stray simultaneous multi-pointer release clears early, but text selection is single-pointer on macOS so this is moot.
- While pointerDown, every selectionchange is suppressed (early return, no timer scheduled) — so keyboard changes made mid-drag also stay suppressed until release.
- On pointerup AND pointercancel (shared endPointerGesture, guarded by 'if (!pointerDown) return'), the settle timer is cleared and reportSelection() runs once. Collapsed selection -> range null -> no 'selection' post (only selection_cleared if one was live). pointercancel is treated like a release.
- Keyboard path: selectionchange with no pointer down debounces SETTLE_MS=300ms then reports once. (Old value was 180ms.)
- element_click unchanged; its 'sel && !sel.isCollapsed' guard still works because pointerup runs before the click handler and, for a plain click, reportSelection sees a collapsed selection and posts nothing.
docs/api.md 'Bridge protocol' selection row updated: timing note changed from 'debounced ~180ms' to gesture-completion semantics (pointer release vs ~300ms keyboard settle).
Verified in a real Chromium (agent-browser) with an opaque-origin sandboxed iframe hosting the real bridge.js: (1) synthetic-event scenarios drag/keyboard/clear/kbd-while-pointer/collapsed-drag/cancel all pass exactly; (2) GENUINE native mouse drag: 0 'selection' while button held (past 300ms), exactly 1 on release with correct anchor+rect; (3) clean native click on a data-cfy-id element still posts element_click with no spurious selection. cargo check + clippy -D warnings + npm run build green; bridge_tag_is_wellformed_inline_script test still passes (no literal script tag). WKWebView-specific pointer timing left for live checkpoint vu1.4 (do-not-launch-app fence).
close_reason: Verified: pointer-gated selection reporting (boolean pointerDown, shared pointerup/pointercancel handler, 300ms keyboard settle); six synthetic scenarios + genuine native CDP drag + native element click all exact in real Chromium; gates green. WKWebView parity deferred to vu1.4 by design.

title: Selection & highlight UX overhaul: release-gated action popover + visible highlights
description: User-reported UX problems with the in-artifact selection/comment flow: (1) highlight contrast is really poor — hard to see what's highlighted, in both saved-comment highlights and during selection; (2) the 'Comment on selection' popover appears WHILE still dragging (before mouseup) because the bridge reports on debounced selectionchange — it should appear only when the mouse is released; (3) the flow should be: text visibly highlights as you drag → on release, a small action popover hovers above/below the selection offering 'Comment', 'Copy', etc. → choosing Comment opens the composer. Goal: make selection-to-comment feel modern, intuitive and obvious (Medium/Notion-style selection toolbar). Child beads cover bridge timing, the two-stage popover, highlight visual contrast, and a live checkpoint.
acceptance_criteria: All child beads closed; live checkpoint passes: no popover during drag, action toolbar on release, comment + copy both work, highlights clearly visible in light and dark themes at a glance.
close_reason: All four children closed; live checkpoint passed all scenarios in both themes.

title: UI/UX polish pass: modern, sleek shell following macOS + web best practices
description: A deliberate design pass over the whole app shell (NOT the artifact design system — that had its own pass in nsy.5 and is good). The shell UI was built feature-first across M1–M7; this bead makes it feel like a polished modern macOS app. Scope: (1) VISUAL LANGUAGE — audit every surface (sidebar, thread list, thread view, comments sidebar, popovers, composers, generation/error views, settings, dialogs) for consistent spacing rhythm, type scale, border/radius/shadow vocabulary, and color tokens; consider adopting the artifact design system's token palette (skill/design-system.css has @cfy:tokens sentinels for exactly this — visual continuity between shell and artifacts is a stated PRD §6 goal); proper macOS feel (window chrome/titlebar treatment, traffic-light spacing, correct native font stack, subtle vibrancy/translucency only if cheap). (2) INTERACTION STATES — every interactive element gets coherent hover/active/focus-visible states; disabled states with cursor + tooltip reasons; loading states (skeletons or spinners) for project/thread/comment fetches; optimistic feel on mutations. (3) EMPTY STATES — first-run (no projects), empty project (no threads), no comments yet: each gets a purposeful, quietly-styled empty state with the next action (not bare text). (4) ACCESSIBILITY/KEYBOARD — focus-visible rings throughout, sensible tab order, Escape closes overlays/popovers consistently, aria-labels on icon-only buttons, contrast meets WCAG AA in both themes. (5) MOTION — small, fast, compositor-friendly transitions (panel slide/fade, chip pulse) with prefers-reduced-motion respected. (6) DETAILS — status chips, badges, moved/stalled indicators visually coherent; scrollbar styling; text truncation with titles; relative timestamps consistent.
design: Use the frontend-design skill for the quality bar. Verify with real screenshots: run the app (isolated-HOME dev pattern) and capture via the WKWebView snapshot harness (see bd notes on conceptify-nsy.5/57l.4 — app windows can't be screencaptured due to TCC, but a WKWebView harness rendering the built frontend, or agent-browser against the vite dev server at localhost:1420, both work for the shell since it's a normal web app outside Tauri APIs — mock/stub tauri invoke if needed for a static review build, or screenshot the dev server with API-driven state). Iterate: capture → judge → refine. Both themes, plus narrow/comfortable window widths. Keep the diff reviewable: styling + small component structure changes; no data-layer rewrites.
acceptance_criteria: Before/after screenshots of every major surface in light AND dark; consistent token-driven styling (no ad-hoc one-off colors/spacings left in components); all interactive elements have visible focus states; the three empty states implemented; reduced-motion respected; npm run build + gates green; a short design-notes section appended to the bead documenting the token/vocabulary decisions.
notes:
DESIGN NOTES (vxc polish pass) — shell token vocabulary & decisions:

TOKENS (src/App.css): adapted from the artifact design system's @cfy:tokens block (skill/design-system.css) for PRD §6 shell/artifact continuity, with two deliberate deltas: (1) shell tokens switch on [data-theme] (theme.ts) instead of prefers-color-scheme so Settings can force appearance; (2) a three-level surface scale well < paper < raised (window chrome/sidebar < content panes < cards/inputs/popovers) — light #f1ede2/#fbf9f4/#fffefb, dark #100d08/#17140f/#221e17 (warm charcoal, not blue-black). Ink/muted/line/accent/mark taken from the artifact palette; muted darkened to #635c4d in light for AA on 'well'. Single terracotta accent (#a34d24 light / #e09863 dark) with accent-strong (hover), accent-ink (accent-colored text on tinted surfaces — darker in light for AA), on-accent (text on solid fill — near-white in light, near-black in dark since the dark accent is light).

STATUS FAMILIES (fg+bg pairs): info=definition blue (open comments, updating, live runs), ok=new muted print green #3e6b44/#9cc79e (ready, answered), warn=insight gold (generating, moved refs, old-version banner, stalled uses neutral), danger=warning red (error, failures, delete). applied=accent (the artifact changed — strongest state gets the accent). ALL fg/bg pairs verified >= 4.5:1 WCAG AA in both themes via contrast script (worst case accent-ink/accent-bg 5.19 dark; light uses accent-strong-derived accent-ink at ~5.7).

TAILWIND WIRING: tokens exposed via @theme inline (bg-paper, text-muted, border-line, rounded-card/ctl, font-serif...) so [data-theme] re-colors everything with NO dark: duplication in components (zero dark: variants remain in tsx). Primitives in @layer components: .cfy-btn (+primary/secondary/ghost/accent-tinted/danger-tinted), .cfy-input (accent focus ring), .cfy-card, .cfy-popover (shadow+pop animation), .cfy-chip, .cfy-label (tracked caps), .cfy-skeleton.

TYPE: UI in system sans (SF); serif (New York via ui-serif) reserved for display moments only — thread title, Settings h1 + section titles, empty-state headings. Radii: 10px cards / 6px controls (matches artifact --cfy-radius / -sm). ::selection uses the artifact highlighter mark color.

A11Y/KEYBOARD: global 2px accent :focus-visible outline; inputs swap it for accent border+glow. Escape now closes Settings overlay, NewThreadComposer, new-project panel (added), plus existing popover/reply/inline-edit escapes. aria-pressed on appearance segmented buttons; icon-only buttons all have aria-labels; truncated project/thread/header titles carry title= attrs. prefers-reduced-motion kills all animations/transitions globally (spinner meaning preserved by adjacent text labels).

EMPTY STATES: first-run (No projects yet + Create a project), empty project (Nothing asked yet + Ask a question), no comments (sentence pointing at selection + composer). Loading states: shimmer skeleton rows in project/thread/comment lists and Settings.

MACOS FEEL DECISIONS: UI chrome is user-select:none (content opts back in via select-text: comment bodies, answers, logs, question); no pointer cursors on buttons/rows (arrow like native macOS); native scrollbars left untouched (macOS overlay scrollbars beat styled always-visible ones); accent-color on native checkboxes; index.html pre-paint background in paper tones (both schemes) so launch never flashes white.

NARROW WIDTHS: project sidebar w-48->w-56(lg), thread list w-60->w-72(lg); comments sidebar shrinks to min-w-60 with the artifact column compressing first (comment triage stays usable, sidebar never clips); thread header row + sidebar action/filter rows wrap.

VERIFIED: vite+agent-browser screenshot loop against a temporary dev-only Tauri IPC mock (removed before close) — populated thread view, comment chains (all statuses, moved badge, v-tag, replies, reply composer), inline Ask-now run state, batch run block, failure panel w/ log tail, generation progress, generation error w/ log, empty states, settings, first-run, 1280px + 700px, light AND dark, focus ring, Escape handlers. Popover styling not exercised live (needs bridge selection events from a real artifact) but composed entirely from exercised primitives. npm run build (tsc+vite) green; no Rust touched.

title: Simple in-app asks are absurdly slow: skill needs effort tiering + progress feed surfaces raw rate_limit noise
description: Evidence (run 8d503820, thread bde61bb4 'the double :: in rust', 2026-07-05): a trivial UC2 syntax question ('what is the :: syntax in rust?') ran 8.5 min before the user gave up and cancelled. The log shows the agent was NOT stuck or rate-limited — it was faithfully executing the full authoring pipeline for a two-paragraph answer: read the whole self-review reference, created a 6-item task list, 22 Bash calls including the complete 4-frame agent-browser screenshot loop (460/900px × light/dark, PNGs read back). Meanwhile the streaming progress feed displayed raw 'rate_limit_event' stream-json lines (status was 'allowed' — purely informational) which read as scary warnings to the user. TWO-PART FIX: (1) EFFORT TIERING in the skill: SKILL.md gets an explicit sizing step before authoring — classify the question (compact: syntax/definition/single-concept → 300-800 words, diagrams only if they genuinely earn their place, no task-list ceremony, and a LIGHTWEIGHT self-review: the full 4-frame loop is only mandatory when the artifact contains hand-authored SVG or generated diagrams; text+Shiki-only artifacts get a single narrow-width dark-mode render or a source-only review — amend references/self-review.md's 'never skip, all four frames' rule accordingly, it currently forbids proportionality); the ask prompt in flows.rs should pass the same hint ('size your effort to the question'). Update the exact-string prompt tests in lockstep, reinstall skill. (2) PROGRESS FEED HYGIENE in the app: filter non-actionable stream events from run-progress display (rate_limit_event with status allowed should not surface at all; genuinely-limited events SHOULD, phrased plainly); show elapsed time and a calm 'complex explanations can take several minutes' hint in the generating view so long runs read as working, not wedged.
design: Timing expectation after fix: compact questions land in ~1-3 min (author + single render or source review + save). The tier decision belongs in the SKILL (the agent judges the question), not in app code — the app-side hint stays generic. Do not remove the full loop for diagram-bearing artifacts; proportionality, not laxity. Progress filtering lives where run-progress events are parsed (runs.rs forward or frontend GenerationView — pick one place; frontend is cheaper and keeps logs complete).
acceptance_criteria: Re-run the exact question from a fresh thread in 'Rust notes': compact artifact saved well under 3 min with 0 validator warnings and sensible quality; no rate_limit noise in the progress feed; a diagram-bearing ask still runs the full 4-frame review; prompt tests + gates green; skill reinstalled.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (Parts 1+2) — all changes in-boundary, gates green (just check; cargo test --workspace 195+22 pass; npm run build pass; skill reinstalled + verified identical to source).

Part 1 (effort tiering):
- skill/SKILL.md: added a 'Size the effort first' SIZING step at the top of section 4 (before research) with crisp COMPACT (300-800w, diagrams only if they earn it, no task-list ceremony, lightweight review) / STANDARD / DEEP tiers and an explicit 'bias to COMPACT when in doubt' rule. Reconciled the 'typically 1,000-2,500 words' quality bullet to reference the tier. Made section 5 pre-save review PROPORTIONAL (full 4-frame loop for diagram-bearing; single narrow-460 dark render for text+Shiki).
- skill/references/self-review.md: replaced the 'run it for every artifact / never skip / all four frames' opening with a 'How much to run — proportional' section (mandatory 4-frame for hand SVG/diagrams; single narrow dark render + pixelWidth check for text-only; source-only fallback if render tooling missing). Kept strong language for the diagram case.
- flows.rs build_ask_prompt: added step 3 'size your effort to the question per the skill's sizing step (compact -> couple of minutes)'; aligned the review step 6 to the proportional rule. Exact-string test updated in lockstep (ask_prompt_exact_for_fixture passes).
- DECISION: did NOT add the sizing hint to answer/apply prompts — answer runs are already per-comment and small ('a short paragraph or two'), and apply runs are bounded by the targeted comments against an existing artifact; neither authors a fresh full artifact, so the compact-vs-deep tradeoff doesn't apply.

Part 2 (progress-feed hygiene) — filtered in ONE place (frontend), logs stay complete on disk:
- runs.rs classify_line: minimal payload tweak — a rate_limit_event carries no subtype, so for that type detail now forwards the nested rate_limit_info as compact JSON (real shape: {status, resetsAt(unix s), rateLimitType, overageStatus, overageResetsAt, isUsingOverage}) instead of the truncated raw line. Falls back to generic path if the field is absent. Test added.
- appStore.ts: new formatRunProgressLine()/formatRateLimit() — the single filter/format seam feeding BOTH the generation panel (recentProgress) and the sidebar (lastProgress). status=='allowed' && !isUsingOverage -> dropped (returns null, nothing surfaces); genuine limiting -> 'Rate limited — waiting for reset HH:MM' (overageResetsAt when isUsingOverage); unparseable/truncated -> dropped (no raw noise). Offline-verified against the real event shapes.
- GenerationView.tsx: added a ticking mm:ss elapsed clock (useElapsedSeconds, keyed on runId so Retry resets it) + the calm hint 'Complex explanations can take several minutes'.
- docs/api.md: documented the rate_limit_event detail exception.

LIVE RE-RUN (faithful headless replication: exact new build_ask_prompt string + default-adapter flags/model=claude-sonnet-5/cwd, real thread 1af78f3e in 'Rust notes'):
- RESULT: artifact v1 saved, thread ready, warningsCount=0, 658-word COMPACT text+Shiki artifact with ZERO diagrams, sensible quality (metas verbatim, clean data-cfy-id headings). Thread kept + titled 'Rust''s :: path syntax'. 1 rate_limit_event occurred -> would be filtered.
- The sizing fix DEMONSTRABLY WORKED: agent self-reported '654 words (within the 300-800 COMPACT target)' and 'text-and-Shiki-only artifact, so a single narrow-width dark-mode render suffices' -> did exactly 1 screenshot render, NOT the 4-frame loop.
- BUT wall-clock was ~8.2 min (492s), NOT the <3min target. duration_api_ms(484749) ~= duration_ms(490377): ~99% is model round-trips across 35 turns.

*** ROOT CAUSE / ESCALATION (belongs to high-complexity-worker — headless-agent permission/security surface, settings.rs, OUT OF THIS BEAD'S SCOPE) ***
The default adapter's allowedTools = [Bash, Edit, Write] OMITS the Read tool. In headless -p mode with no interactive approval, EVERY Read call is permission-denied ('Claude requested permissions to read from X, but you haven''t granted'). Consequences observed live:
  1. The agent must cat every skill file (spec/design-system/rendering/self-review) — turn-expensive, and it burns extra turns discovering Read is blocked + probing (MEMORY.md, nonexistent.txt).
  2. It CANNOT Read the review PNG (w460-dark.png Read denied twice) — so the visual self-review's 'Read the PNG and judge it' is impossible in the real app; the agent falls back to the mechanical pixelWidth overflow check + source review only. (The single-frame proportional review is correct as spec but its image-inspection step is moot headlessly; overflow is still caught mechanically, and token-only artifacts have low dark-contrast risk, so quality held.)
This is the dominant reason the timing target is missed and is a general headless-run inefficiency (applies to answer/apply runs too). RECOMMENDED FOLLOW-UP (high-complexity, security review): add Read (read-only) to the adapter allowedTools so headless runs stop cat-ing/probing and the visual review can see its renders; optionally also let COMPACT runs skim rendering.md (Shiki recipe only, skip d2/graphviz) to shave further turns. Estimate: removing the ~10-12 Read-denial-friction turns alone would bring a compact run to ~5-5.5min; the reference-skim + Read fix together are what get compact to ~1-3min. Filed as a design decision rather than fixed here because it touches the agent's file-access security surface (settings.rs default_adapters), which is explicitly out of this bead's file boundary and reserved for high-complexity review.

Leaving bead in_progress: Parts 1+2 done & verified; the <3min acceptance is blocked on the Read-permission follow-up above.
Orchestrator: applied the escalated settings fix directly — Read/Glob/Grep added to allowedTools (read-only, strictly weaker than allowed Bash; zero containment cost, removes the 35-turn permission-friction found in the live run). Pin tests updated (30→33 args). The <3min acceptance re-verification is folded into the M7 checkpoint bead (conceptify-6xi.6), which runs real agent runs anyway.
close_reason: Fixed: sizing step + proportional review in skill; prompt hint; rate-limit noise filtered + elapsed clock; Read/Glob/Grep allowed. Timing re-verification delegated to 6xi.6 checkpoint.

title: M7 checkpoint: conversational loop end-to-end live
description: Live integration checkpoint on a real artifact with real agent runs (modest count, like b12.9): (1) comment on a section → Ask now → answer lands inline; (2) reply 'I still don't get …' → root re-opens → Ask now → the new answer demonstrably builds on the exchange (references the prior answer's framing, doesn't repeat it); (3) batch Ask follow-ups with a mix of fresh roots and a re-opened chained root → all answered correctly per-row; (4) apply one chained comment → chain freezes applied, other comments re-anchor onto the new version; (5) cancel + failure inline paths. Fix small issues found; file beads sparingly for anything deeper. Rebuild/refresh /Applications + install-cli afterward so the user runs the finished feature.
acceptance_criteria: All five scenarios verified live with evidence (comment/run ids, DB states, event order); the reply answer visibly uses exchange context; gates green; fresh bundle + CLI installed.
notes:
M7 LIVE CHECKPOINT RESULTS (real HOME, real DB, real headless agents; driven via the b12.9-style TEMPORARY debug-only bearer-authed HTTP harness — src-tauri/src/server/debug_flows_routes.rs, debug_assertions-gated, mounted inside the protected router — under 'npm run tauri dev'; REMOVED after the checkpoint, git diff verified clean of it, and the live release app 404s the routes).

TARGET: meta thread d1992afa (How Conceptify works, end to end), starting at artifact v2. Event verification via a listen_any tap (comment-created/updated, artifact-updated, thread-updated, run-finished, run-progress) appending to a file.

S1 FRESH ROOT -> ASK NOW: element-anchored root d0d2c31c (fig-launch-wait, 'two publishes race' question) via POST /comments -> ask_single_comment run 2265f615 (haiku, mode=answer, target_comment_ids=[root]) -> 30s wall (15:42:47->15:43:17). Event order: comment-created -> 45x run-progress -> comment-updated{answered} -> run-finished{completed}. answer_html substantive AND factually correct (single-instance plugin + bind_with_fallback health-probe defer — both verified against source). FR-4.9 verified live mid-run: concurrent ask_single AND ask_follow_ups both rejected 'already has an active run (2265f615…)'.

S2 REPLY -> RE-OPEN -> ASK NOW: reply 13eda6eb (parent_id=d0d2c31c) -> comment-created(reply) + comment-updated(root, status open) fired 1ms apart; root open, resolved_at cleared. ask_single_comment run 6a0d9f58 -> 40s. Answer landed ON THE REPLY ROW (reply answered, resolved_at stamped); root's original answer_html byte-identical untouched. The reply answer demonstrably builds on the exchange: picks up the 'second process stands down' framing from answer #1 and answers only the new questions (CLI outcome + port-file cleanup) — zero repetition.

DEFECT FOUND + FIXED (root status semantics): answering a reply left the re-opened ROOT 'open' forever — stale Ask-now affordance, inflated open counts, and the next batch run would re-target the root and OVERWRITE its original answer (resolve_target falls back to the root when all replies are answered). Fix per the epic's own design line 'root status reflects the latest exchange state': comments::update_comment now flips the parent root open->answered in the same tx when the answered reply is the chain's LATEST message (root answer_html untouched, resolved_at re-stamped via the same first-resolution CASE; answering an earlier/superseded reply does NOT flip). CommentContext gained answered_root; PATCH route emits a second comment-updated for the root. Files: comments.rs (+3 unit tests incl. full reopen->flip loop + non-latest-reply negative), comments_routes.rs (+route test asserting both events), flows.rs (2 existing tests updated to the new contract — a fully-answered chain root is now a legitimate apply-all default target, asserted explicitly with a still-open root excluded), docs/api.md event-table note. No frontend change needed (comment-updated -> refetchComments already handles it).

S3 BATCH MIXED SET: fresh anchored root c01b3917 (fig-code-savetx, save-tx-vs-pointer crash question) + second reply d6db1f0a on the S2 chain + pre-existing open root 21fb8027 rode along -> ask_follow_ups run a105f614: target_comment_ids = exactly the 3 open ROOTS (replies never separate targets). 82s. Answers landed one-by-one (15:55:10.8 / 13.0 / 15.2). Chain: reply#2 answered AND root flip comment-updated at the identical ms (same tx) — THE FIX VERIFIED LIVE IN A REAL AGENT RUN. Root's original answer preserved; reply#1 untouched; reply#2's answer builds on the chain (port-file-as-truth reasoning extending the 4487-fallback thread).

S4 APPLY CHAINED ROOT: apply_to_artifact([d0d2c31c]) run 49b69333 (sonnet) 201s completed -> v3 created (created_by=follow_up), artifact.html pointer == v3 bytes, cfy:version meta=3. CHAIN FROZE: root d0d2c31c applied@v2, replies 13eda6eb+d6db1f0a stay answered@v2 (re-attachment excludes replies). Other roots re-anchored v2->v3 all 'anchored' (cfy_ids survived); old applied e54d74c9 stays @v1. Event order EXACTLY per contract: thread-updated{updating} -> comment-updated{applied} (before save) -> artifact-updated{version:3} -> 4x re-attachment comment-updated -> run-finished{completed}; thread back 'ready' (silent flip by design).

S5 CANCEL + FAILURE: throwaway open root -> ask_single run 2a65ade6 cancelled ~4s in -> status=cancelled, run-finished{cancelled} event, comment still open with NULL answer — no corruption. Failure: agent_binary_path=/tmp/definitely-not-an-agent-binary -> ask_single returned the error string to the caller ('io error: No such file or directory') AND left run row 4a0ee892 failed with started_at==finished_at + '[run] ABNORMAL END: failed to spawn' in the log. NOTE (expected, not a defect): spawn-failure emits NO run-finished event — the start invoke itself errors, so the UI surfaces it via the existing actionError path and never has an activeRun; run-finished{failed} is for runs that started then died (covered by unit tests + b12.9). Settings restored (override cleared; settings table back to 0 rows = zero-config baseline).

S6 COMPACT-ASK TIMING (folded-in conceptify-pri re-verification): ask_from_app 'What does the ? operator do in Rust?' in Rust notes -> run cea32630 (sonnet) COMPLETED IN 3m15s (16:02:04->16:05:19), num_turns=30, agent result: 'Published cleanly as version 1 with zero warnings'. ZERO permission denials in the log (Read/Glob/Grep allowlist fix working), 2 rate_limit_events both status=allowed (filtered from UI feed per pri part 2), artifact compact: ~850 words incl. code tokens, ZERO svg/diagrams. VERDICT: pri prediction (~2-4min vs old 8.5min) CONFIRMED; under the 5-min threshold; no new bead filed. Thread kept ('What does the ? operator do in Rust?', ready).

6xi.4's UI scenarios verified structurally (pixels not capturable headlessly): (1) single-run distinction — RunStartedDto mode=answer + 1 target id observed live, feeding inlineRunRootId; (2) reply-row resolve + live flip events observed; (3) cancel event clears activeRun; (4) start-failure surfaces via actionError string (verbatim FlowError text confirmed); (5)/(6) reload + filter fallbacks remain as reasoned in 6xi.4 (ActiveRun DTO carries no target ids — confirmed in commands.rs).

GATES: just check green, cargo test --workspace 224+23 green (2 flow tests updated to new contract, 4 tests added), npm run build green. WRAP-UP: harness removed (working tree holds ONLY the root-flip fix + tests + docs); release rebuilt POST-FIX, ditto'd to /Applications, install-cli re-linked, conceptify doctor ok:true; fresh release app RUNNING and navigated to the meta thread (POST /open); throwaway probe comment deleted; genuinely-useful Q&A (launch-race chain incl. frozen applied conversation, save-tx question, re-attachment-cost answer) kept for reading. NOT committed, bead left open per orchestrator instruction.

title: Ask now button: per-comment immediate run + inline run state

Invokes ask_single_comment; while that run is active show compact inline state on THAT root (spinner + cancel via existing cancel_run) instead of/alongside the sidebar's batch run block — scope the existing run-status block so a single-comment run doesn't render as a batch (get_active_run/target ids already expose enough to distinguish; extend the run state slice minimally if needed). Disabled states per FR-4.9 (any active run on the thread disables all Ask now buttons + batch button, with tooltip). Failure surfaces the existing log-tail panel pattern scoped to the comment.
acceptance_criteria: Ask now on an open root spawns a single-target run with inline spinner/cancel on that comment; batch button and other Ask now buttons disable during it; answer lands threaded and root flips answered live; cancel and failure paths surface inline; npm run build + gates green.
notes:
6xi.4 frontend implemented (orchestrator to close/commit). Files: src/lib/api.ts, src/store/appStore.ts, src/components/CommentsSidebar.tsx. npm run build green (tsc+vite). src-tauri untouched.

SINGLE-vs-BATCH DISTINCTION (CommentsSidebar): inlineRunRootId = sidebarRun.mode==='answer' && targetIds?.length===1 ? targetIds[0] : null. Such a run renders a compact InlineRunState (spinner + 'Answering…' + icon cancel wired to cancelActiveRun/cancel_run) ON that root's chain, replacing its action slot; headerRun (the batch RunStatusBlock) is suppressed for it. A batch 'Ask follow-ups' with exactly one open comment is ALSO length-1 and is deliberately shown the same inline way (it IS answering that one root — coherent, no functional issue). title: Threaded sidebar: reply composer + chain rendering
description: Sidebar UX for conversations (CommentsSidebar.tsx + store): render each root comment with its reply chain nested one level (ordered), each exchange message showing body/answer_html + per-row status; a Reply affordance on answered/applied roots opens an inline composer (Cmd/Ctrl+Enter submits, same conventions as the popover/composer) → createComment with parent_id → root visibly re-opens (status chip flips via the re-open event). Filters keep operating on ROOT status; counts unchanged. Anchor interactions (scroll/highlight/moved badge) remain root-only. Store: comments slice groups by parent_id (roots + chains), addComment handles replies; events.ts needs no new events (comment-created/updated already fire). Keep visual hierarchy quiet — replies indented, smaller meta, consistent with existing Tailwind styling.
acceptance_criteria: Chains render nested and ordered under roots; reply composer creates a reply and the root re-opens live; filters/counts still root-based; anchor click/scroll unaffected by replies; npm run build green; live DB-observable verification of the create-reply path per prior beads' isolated-HOME pattern.
notes:
6xi.3 frontend implemented (orchestrator to close/commit). Files: src/lib/api.ts, src/store/appStore.ts, src/components/CommentsSidebar.tsx.

GROUPING: comments slice stays FLAT in the store (single source of truth for highlights). Added derived pure fn groupComments(comments): CommentChain[] in appStore.ts (exported, with exported CommentChain{root,replies}). title: Single-comment runs + exchange-history prompts (Ask now backend)
description: Flow layer for Ask now (flows.rs): ask_single_comment(thread_id, root_comment_id) — answer-mode run targeting ONE root comment (open or re-opened), reusing the engine, guard (one run per thread, FR-4.9), child-PATH prep, and watcher patterns from ask_follow_ups. Apply this history block to the BATCH prompt too (ask_follow_ups) so re-opened roots get context in batch runs. title: Replies backend: parent_id model, re-open semantics, API/CLI surface
description: Data model + API for threaded replies. Append migration #10: comments.parent_id TEXT NULL REFERENCES comments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE (a reply dies with its root; thread cascade already covers both). Domain rules in comments.rs: a reply must target a comment in the same thread whose parent_id IS NULL (linear chains — replying to a reply is rejected, reply to the root instead); replies carry no anchor (NULL, and anchor_state stays 'anchored'/ignored); creating a USER reply on an answered/applied root flips the root back to open (one UPDATE, emits comment-updated) so FR-4.6 batch gathering and open-comment counts work unchanged; agent answers to a reply land via the existing resolve path ON THE REPLY row (replies have their own status open→answered; applied is root-only). Extend: POST /api/v1/comments accepts parent_id; list endpoints + list_comments/create_comment commands + CLI list-comments pass parent_id through; get-context/context.rs nests reply chains (ordered created_at,rowid) under each open root so flows inherit exchange history with no prompt changes yet. Docs: comments section of docs/api.md (reply rules, re-open semantics), docs/cli.md field note.
design: Root status = conversation state (open means 'needs agent attention'), reply status = per-message state. Rejecting reply-to-reply keeps chains linear per the epic. Open-comment count semantics unchanged (counts open roots only — verify the threads-list JOIN excludes parent_id IS NOT NULL; adjust if replies would double-count).
acceptance_criteria: Reply rows persist with parent_id + null anchor; reply-to-reply rejected 400; user reply re-opens an answered root (event fired); resolve-comment answers a reply; list APIs and get-context expose chains nested/ordered; threads-list open counts unaffected by replies; migration test proves old rows survive; full gates green.
notes:
6xi.1 backend implemented (orchestrator to close/commit). Key decisions for 6xi.2/6xi.3:

MIGRATION 10 (COMMENT_PARENT_ID): plain ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN parent_id TEXT NULL REFERENCES comments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE + CREATE INDEX idx_comments_parent_id. NOT a table rebuild — SQLite permits an ALTER-added column with a REFERENCES clause when its default is NULL (a nullable no-DEFAULT column), and nothing about existing columns changes (unlike the ask-mode #9 rebuild). Existing comments become roots (parent_id NULL). migrations.rs test constant refactor: LATEST=10, new ASK_MODE=9 pins the ask-mode rebuild boundary so future appends don't shift it. New migration test proves old comment rows survive + self-ref FK/cascade + index.

FLOWS.RS BOUNDARY (important): to honor 'do not touch flows.rs' (it constructs a Comment{} literal, calls create_comment 5-arg, and reads ThreadContext.open_comments: Vec<Comment>), I did NOT add parent_id to the Comment struct or change create_comment/list_comments/open_comments signatures. Instead parent_id rides alongside via additive paths: new comments::create_reply(conn, thread_id, parent_id, body); CommentContext gained parent_id + reopened_root fields; list_comments_with_parent() returns Vec<(Comment,Option<String>)> (list_comments delegates to it); to_response(comment, parent_id); ThreadContext gained open_comment_threads: Vec<CommentThread> (open_comments Vec<Comment> kept unchanged for flows). If 6xi.2 refactors flows.rs anyway, it may consolidate parent_id onto Comment then.

REPLY RULES (comments::create_reply): reply carries NO anchor (route/command reject anchor+parent_id -> CommentError::ReplyWithAnchor 400); parent must exist (ParentNotFound 404), be same thread (ParentDifferentThread 400), be a root (ReplyToReply 400, linear chains). Reply inherits parent's artifact_version (caller value ignored), status open, anchor_state anchored.

RE-OPEN: a user reply on an answered/applied root flips ROOT to open in the same tx: UPDATE status='open', resolved_at=NULL, answer_html KEPT (exchange history). Bypasses the advance-only status machine deliberately. Route emits comment-updated(root, status=open) IN ADDITION to comment-created(reply). Cleared resolved_at preserves the 'open => resolved_at NULL' invariant so the next answer re-stamps it.

REPLY STATUS: replies advance open->answered via the normal PATCH path on the reply row. applied is root-only: update_comment rejects AppliedOnReply (400) when target=applied and comment has a parent.

COUNT FIX (threads.rs): open_comment_count now SUM(CASE WHEN status='open' AND parent_id IS NULL) — counts open ROOTS only, so a re-opened root + its open replies count once.

RE-ATTACHMENT: reattach_candidates gained AND parent_id IS NULL — replies never participate (no anchor, inherit version). anchoring.rs unchanged (it only iterates candidates).

CONTEXT JSON SHAPE (get-context nesting): ThreadContextResponse.open_comments is now Vec<ThreadContextComment> where ThreadContextComment = { #[serde(flatten)] comment: CommentResponse, replies: Vec<CommentResponse> }. i.e. each open ROOT is a normal comment object + a 'replies' array (ordered created_at,rowid; roots parent_id null, replies parent_id=root id). CLI get-context mirrors this (camelCase, nested 'replies'). Documented in docs/api.md + docs/cli.md. CommentResponse/CommentDto/CLI gained parent_id/parentId.

HEADS-UP for 6xi.2 (flows.rs, off-limits to me): (1) ctx.open_comments (flat) now includes open REPLIES as well as open roots — ask_follow_ups uses it as batch targets, so open replies become answer targets (may be desired, but be deliberate). Use ctx.open_comment_threads for the nested exchange history in prompts. (2) apply_to_artifact selects Answered comments as targets; an Answered REPLY would be picked but resolve-comment --applied on it now returns 400 (AppliedOnReply) — filter targets to roots (parent_id IS NULL) in the apply flow.

TEST-SCHEMA RIPPLE: added 'parent_id TEXT REFERENCES comments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE' to the hand-written comments test schemas in comments.rs, threads.rs, artifacts.rs, and artifact_protocol.rs (reattach_candidates now references parent_id). artifact_protocol.rs is not in my listed files but is not off-limits and this was a mechanical necessity.

GATES: just check (clippy -D warnings) green; cargo test green for every module I touched (comments/threads/context/server routes/migrations/artifacts/artifact_protocol/commands + lib integration + CLI + types); npm run build green. The only 2 cargo-test-workspace failures are settings.rs tests from a CONCURRENT agent's in-flight change (default allowedTools now Bash/Edit/Write/Read/Glob/Grep vs test expecting Bash/Edit/Write) — not from this bead (settings.rs untouched by me).

title: [epic] M7 — Conversational interrogation: threaded replies + per-comment Ask now
description: Make the crown-jewel loop iterative instead of one-shot (extends PRD §7.4/UC4; picked as the top post-Phase-1 improvement). Today: comment → one answer → done; if the answer doesn't click there is no in-place 'I still don't get why X', and a single burning question must wait for a batch run. This epic adds (a) THREADED REPLIES: a reply box on an answered/applied comment; the follow-up run receives the full exchange history (original comment + answer + replies) so answers build on each other; and (b) PER-COMMENT 'ASK NOW': fire a single-comment answer run immediately without gathering the whole batch. Batch 'Ask follow-ups' (FR-4.6) stays; Ask now complements it. Apply mode (FR-4.7) is untouched except replies freeze with their parent.
design: Reply = a comments row with parent_id (new column, append-only migration #10). Replies inherit the parent's anchor (no own anchor; anchor_state lives on the root only). A comment chain is LINEAR (a conversation under one root comment), not a tree — one level of nesting in UI, ordered by created_at,rowid. Root comment status reflects the latest exchange state: replying to an answered root re-opens it (status back to open) so batch runs and open-comment counts keep working unchanged. Exchange history enters prompts via context aggregation (context.rs) — get-context gains reply chains under each open comment. One-run-per-thread guard (FR-4.9) applies to Ask now too.
acceptance_criteria: Live on a real artifact: comment → answer → reply ('I still don't get…') → Ask now → a contextual answer that references the prior exchange lands threaded in the sidebar; batch flow still answers roots including re-opened ones; apply flow still works with reply chains present; all existing tests green.

title: Ask from Conceptify: composer + streaming progress (FR-5.1, FR-5.2)
description: Self-sufficient asking (PRD §7.5, UC5): 'New thread' in a project → question composer → spawns a headless agent (adapter + models.inAppAsk, cwd = project root) instructed to author per the artifact spec and publish via the CLI. While generating: streaming progress view parsing stream-json events (current activity, tool use) in the thread view (FR-2.3), swapping to the artifact viewer the moment save-artifact lands.
acceptance_criteria: Type a question in-app → watch live agent activity → artifact appears without touching a terminal; thread status transitions generating→ready; works in an auto-created (non-repo) topic project.
notes: 959.1/959.2 impl decisions (medium-worker): (1) completed-without-artifact = error. The ask watcher, on ANY terminal run outcome (completed/failed/timeout/cancelled), does a single conditional transition generating->error. This surfaces crash/timeout/cancel AND catches an exit-0 run that never saved (thread still 'generating' -> 'error'), while never regressing a 'ready' that the agent's mid-run save-artifact set (conditional no-ops from 'ready'). Same race-free pattern as the apply watcher's updating->ready revert. (2) Status flow: create_thread sets 'generating'; save-artifact (via CLI->POST route->artifacts::save_artifact) sets 'ready' + emits artifact-updated -> viewer swaps in. No 'updating' for ask. (3) start-failure hygiene (N4): if start_run fails after the thread row exists (CLI-not-found, cwd-missing, spawn-fail), the thread is flipped generating->error so it's never stuck 'generating'. retry sets 'generating' BEFORE start (closes the finish-vs-set race) and reverts to error if start fails. (4) Added flows::latest_run_for_thread + get_latest_run command so the thread-view error state can always resolve the failed run id (log tail + retry) even after app restart.

title: Run status UI: progress, cancel, failure surfacing (FR-4.8)
description: Visibility into in-flight runs (PRD FR-4.8): sidebar shows the active run — spinner, per-comment progress (which are answered so far), cancel button that kills the process. Failures surface the log tail inline; full runs/<id>.log retained and openable for debugging. Covers both answer-mode and apply-mode runs.

notes:
IMPLEMENTED (high-worker; NOT closed/committed per orchestrator).

RUN STATUS UI (CommentsSidebar, covers answer + apply runs): the actions row swaps to a RunStatusBlock while a run is active — spinner + mode label ('Answering follow-ups…' / 'Applying to artifact…'), per-comment progress 'n of m answered|applied' DERIVED from store comment statuses against the run's target-id set (answer: target left open; apply: target reached applied) so each comment-updated refetch advances it with no separate counter to drift, latest run-progress detail line (mono, truncated), and Cancel -> appStore.cancelActiveRun() -> existing cancel_run command (SIGKILL process group; partial answers preserved; authoritative 'cancelled' arrives via run-finished).

STORE SLICE (appStore): activeRun {runId, threadId, mode, targetIds|null, lastProgress} + runFailure {runId, threadId, status failed|timeout}. Fed by run-progress/run-finished listeners added centrally in events.ts. run-finished: clears activeRun, refetches comments+threads, sets runFailure for failed|timeout (uniform FR-5.3 error class; message says which). Thread switch: cleared with the viewer slice, then syncActiveRun(threadId) -> NEW get_active_run command (registry liveness + DB mode via flows::active_run_summary; status always 'running' since terminal runs leave the registry before run-finished) — re-attached runs have targetIds=null (not persisted) -> indeterminate spinner, documented. run-progress for the selected thread with no tracked run also triggers a re-attach (covers runs started before selection).

FAILURE SURFACING: RunFailurePanel (shown when runFailure set and no active run): failure-class message, 'Show log' loads tail on demand via NEW get_run_log_tail command (#[tauri::command], commands.rs conventions: SELECT log_path FROM follow_up_runs, flows::tail_lines(path, max_lines.unwrap_or(30)); unreadable file degrades to one explanatory line so the panel ALWAYS shows log_path), scrollable <pre> tail + full path displayed, Dismiss. Full runs/<id>.log retained on disk (engine behavior, unchanged). Run history beyond latest run: out of scope per bead.

The prompts already carry the load-bearing contract (per-comment resolve-comment; answer files = HTML fragment or markdown, no html/head/body wrapper; apply ordering all-edits -> all --applied -> one save-artifact; cfy-id immutability; cfy:src regeneration; cfy:version bump). follow-ups.md should DEEPEN, not contradict: answer style/tone/length, when to include code snippets, how to read anchors (verbatim snake_case inner keys), diagram re-render specifics per renderer, and that anchor.quote/cfy_id stability is the re-attachment contract (per b12.3 notes).

title: Apply to artifact: explicit second step (FR-4.7)
description: Live artifact evolution (PRD FR-4.7, UC4): per answered comment — or batch — 'Apply to artifact' spawns a run in apply mode (models.artifactUpdate): the agent edits/regenerates the relevant artifact portions (using embedded diagram DSL sources per FR-3.4 rather than hand-editing SVG; keeping existing data-cfy-ids stable), saves a NEW version via save-artifact, the viewer refreshes live, and the comments transition to 'applied'. Re-anchoring (M4) handles remaining comments against the new version. Simple Q&A never needs this mode — it exists for 'make this clearer' feedback.
acceptance_criteria: Apply on an answered comment produces artifact v(n+1), live viewer refresh, comment status 'applied'; other comments re-anchor or get 'reference moved'; diagram-targeted apply regenerates from embedded DSL source; thread status updating→ready throughout.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (high-worker; NOT closed/committed per orchestrator). Same file set as b12.4 (flows.rs is the shared home).

APPLY-ORDERING DECISION (the crux, per 94m.7's note): PROMPT-ENFORCED, not server-enforced. The apply prompt's contract, in order: (1) copy the artifact to a working file (never edit the app-owned file in place); (2) make ALL edits there — data-cfy-ids immutable, diagrams regenerated from the embedded <!--cfy:src--> DSL (never hand-edit SVG), bump <meta name=cfy:version> to n+1, stay self-contained; (3) when final, for EACH target comment: resolve-comment --id <id> --answer-file <brief change note> --applied; (4) THEN save-artifact exactly once, as the very last CLI call. The prompt EXPLAINS why (agents follow rules they understand): --applied freezes each comment at its capture version so the save's re-attachment pass migrates only untouched comments. Rejected alternative: marking targets applied server-side before/at spawn would lie in the DB if the run fails. Residual accepted imprecision: a run dying between step 3 and 4 leaves comments 'applied' whose fix never published — visible via the loud FR-4.8 failure panel; a comment's applied status is honest about agent intent, and 'do NOT save or mark applied if you could not complete the edits' is a hard rule. Misbehaving agent that saves first degrades gracefully (re-attachment processes not-yet-applied targets — noisy, never corrupting).

TARGET SEMANTICS: apply_to_artifact(thread_id, comment_ids); empty ids = all status=answered comments; explicit ids must exist on the thread and be open|answered (open->applied one-shot is legal and tested; applied -> AlreadyApplied error). NoTargetComments when nothing qualifies.

THREAD STATUS (PRD §4, decided + documented in flows.rs module docs + docs/api.md): apply runs OWN 'updating' — set unconditionally after start_run succeeds (a rejected start never touches status), emit thread-updated {project_id, thread_id, status}. A watcher awaits StartedRun.finished and runs threads::transition_thread_status(updating -> ready) — a NEW conditional single-statement UPDATE appended to threads.rs — so a 'ready' already set by the agent's mid-run save-artifact is never regressed (check+write atomic); emits thread-updated ready only when a row changed. Applies to ALL terminal statuses incl. cancelled; NEVER 'error' (generation-only, FR-5.3/M6). New 'thread-updated' event wired in events.ts -> refetchThreads(project_id) + refetchProjects, so status chips go live (thread lists previously only refetched on thread-created).

UI: per-answered-comment 'Apply to artifact' button (CommentRow footer) + header 'Apply all answered (n)' batch button; both disabled during any active run / without an artifact. Viewer refresh needs nothing new: agent's save-artifact -> HTTP route -> artifact-updated -> existing handleArtifactUpdated path; comment applied transitions ride comment-updated.

TESTS: apply_prompt_exact_for_fixture (full exact string incl. ordering section), apply_defaults_to_answered_sets_updating_then_ready (targets exclude open+applied; updating set before return + event; watcher restores ready + event; prompt contains only the answered id, '--applied', 'mark the comments applied FIRST', save-artifact line), apply_failure_restores_ready_and_never_error (exit 3 -> run failed, thread ready, exactly updating+ready events), apply_validates_targets (NoTargetComments/CommentNotFound/AlreadyApplied/open-id-legal), threads.rs transition_is_conditional_on_current_status. NOT exercised: a real agent actually calling resolve-comment+save-artifact in order (needs a live HTTP server + real CLI — the CLI->PATCH->comment-updated and save->re-attachment paths are already integration-tested in their own modules; the ordering itself is prompt text, verified by the exact-string test). Live UC4 pass = b12.9.

title: Ask follow-ups: batch sidebar-first flow (FR-4.6) + concurrency guard (FR-4.9)
description: The interrogation loop's answer half (PRD FR-4.6, UC4): 'Ask follow-ups' button gathers ALL open comments → spawns ONE headless run (adapter + models.followUp, cwd = project root) with a prompt assembled from get-context → the agent answers EACH comment individually via resolve-comment → sidebar updates live per-comment as answers land. NO artifact modification in this mode — answering and applying are deliberately separate steps. FR-4.9: enforce one follow-up run per thread at a time (button disabled + API guard while a run is active).
acceptance_criteria: With 3 open comments (mixed text/diagram/direct), one click produces 3 individual sidebar answers landing incrementally; artifact file untouched (same version); starting a second run on the same thread is rejected while the first is active; thread status correct throughout.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (high-worker; NOT closed/committed per orchestrator).

FILES: src-tauri/src/flows.rs (NEW: flow layer — prompt assembly, child env, guards, thread-status policy; 9 tests), src-tauri/src/runs.rs (minimal engine extension: StartRun.env Vec<(String,String)> applied via cmd.env before spawn — the engine genuinely lacked any way to hand the child a PATH; stays policy-free, flows decide WHAT to set; module-level allow(dead_code) dropped now that flows consume the surface), src-tauri/src/commands.rs (appended ask_follow_ups/apply_to_artifact/get_active_run/get_run_log_tail wrappers + DTOs), src-tauri/src/lib.rs (mod flows + 4 command registrations), src/lib/api.ts + src/store/appStore.ts + src/lib/events.ts + src/components/{CommentsSidebar,ThreadView}.tsx, docs/api.md ('Flow commands' section + thread-updated event row).

FR-4.6 FLOW: ask_follow_ups(thread_id) -> context::thread_context under one lock -> guards (NoArtifact / NoOpenComments) -> build_answer_prompt (pure fn, exact-string unit-tested) -> child_env -> runs::start_run(mode=Answer, Purpose::FollowUp via RunMode.purpose(), cwd=project root via adapter template). Returns {run_id, mode, target_comment_ids} — target ids are NOT persisted; only the starting session gets per-comment progress, a re-attached session (get_active_run) shows indeterminate spinner. Answers land per-comment via the existing PATCH /comments -> comment-updated -> refetch path; the flow needs no completion watcher for answer mode and NEVER touches thread status (FR-5.3-lite: failed/timeout surfaces in the run UI only, no thread 'error' — reserved for generation runs/M6).

FR-4.9 GUARD: engine registry reservation is authoritative (unchanged); UI half = sidebar buttons disabled while activeRun != null (+ a 'starting' latch bridging the click->activeRun gap); a raced second start surfaces the engine's AlreadyRunning as an inline sidebar error.

CHILD PATH (§5.1 GUI PATH problem) DECISION: flows resolve the conceptify CLI once per process — precedence CONCEPTIFY_CLI env override (tests/escape hatch) -> sibling of current_exe (dev builds: both workspace binaries in target/<profile>) -> settings::resolve_agent_binary("conceptify", None) i.e. cached login-shell which (the just install-cli symlink) — and prepend its parent dir to the child PATH via StartRun.env (dedup if already a component; empty inherited PATH falls back to the macOS GUI default /usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin). Resolution failure aborts BEFORE any run row exists with an actionable message. Tested end-to-end: fake agent runs 'command -v conceptify' in the child and resolves the stub.

ANSWER PROMPT CONTRACT (what the agent sees): identity + context (project root, thread title/id, question, absolute artifact path+version), comments as pretty-JSON array {id, artifactVersion, status, anchor VERBATIM (inner keys snake_case, same passthrough contract as get-context), body, answerHtml}, exact CLI steps (mktemp -d scratch dir; one answer file + one resolve-comment per comment, resolve as soon as each answer is ready so sidebar fills incrementally), hard rules (never save-artifact, never --applied), and a defensive pointer to ~/.claude/skills/conceptify/references/follow-ups.md 'if it exists' (b12.7 writes it). serde_json Value maps alphabetize keys -> deterministic prompt, exact-string tested.

TESTS (flows.rs): answer_prompt_exact_for_fixture (full exact string), ask_follow_ups_spawns_answer_run_with_prompt_and_cli_on_path (prompt content, child PATH + command -v conceptify, mode/agent row, thread status untouched, zero thread-updated events), ask_follow_ups_guards_... (NoArtifact, NoOpenComments, AlreadyRunning for BOTH flows, active_run_summary through registry+DB, guard release after cancel), prepend_path unit tests. title: get-context + resolve-comment: API + CLI (the headless agent's I/O)
description: How headless agents read and write (PRD §5.2, §5.5): 'conceptify get-context --thread <id>' → thread + question + artifact path + open comments with anchors (JSON) — the run-specific context Conceptify assembles into the prompt; 'conceptify list-comments --thread <id> [--status open]'; 'conceptify resolve-comment --id <id> --answer-file <path> [--applied]' → marks answered/applied, stores answer_html, updates the sidebar live via comment-resolved event. These are the contract FR-6.4 teaches.
acceptance_criteria: get-context output contains everything an agent needs to answer comments without DB access; resolve-comment transitions status and the sidebar updates live; --applied variant sets applied status; answer file may be markdown or HTML fragment and renders correctly in the sidebar.
notes:
b12.3 implemented (medium-worker). Kept in_progress for orchestrator to close.

RESOLVE-COMMENT REUSES EXISTING ENDPOINT — no new API. PATCH /api/v1/comments/:id already does everything: status advance (answered/applied), answer_html, emits comment-updated. CLI resolve-comment is a pure wrapper: reads --answer-file CLI-side and sends it VERBATIM as answer_html (default status=answered; --applied => applied, using the legal open->applied one-shot). Answer file may be an HTML fragment OR markdown — passed through unchanged; the SIDEBAR (frontend, not mine) must render answer_html which per FR-4.5 may be markdown or HTML. Flag for frontend/b12.7.

GET-CONTEXT AGGREGATION IS A REUSABLE RUST DOMAIN FN (for b12.2). New module src-tauri/src/context.rs: pub fn thread_context(conn, thread_id) -> Result<ThreadContext, ContextError>. Composes thread + project + artifacts::latest_artifact + open comments (comments::list_comments status=Open) under one conn lock. The HTTP route (GET /api/v1/threads/:id/context in server/threads_routes.rs) just maps it to conceptify_types::ThreadContextResponse. b12.2 spawner should call context::thread_context DIRECTLY for server-side prompt assembly — do NOT shell out to the get-context CLI. ContextError::ThreadNotFound => 404.

New helpers (append-only): artifacts::latest_artifact + LatestArtifact{version,file_path}; threads::get_thread_opt (pub, refactored out of get_thread) + ThreadError::ThreadNotFound; comments_routes::to_response made pub(super) so the context route serves comments identically to GET /comments. lib.rs: single additive 'mod context;'.

EXACT SHAPES.
