title: OQ2: artifact storage location — in-repo .conceptify/ vs centralized
description: Open question 2 (PRD §12): where do artifact files live? Option A (current §5.6 assumption): <project_root>/.conceptify/threads/<slug>/artifact.vN.html — travels with the repo, human-visible, but pollutes it (needs global-gitignore suggestion; skill must never commit it). Option B: centralized ~/Documents/conceptify/artifacts/<project>/ — clean repos, less portable. PRD calls this 'cheap to flip in M0 — decide before M2' because the save-artifact storage layout (M2) bakes it in. Decide and record the rationale here; update §5.6 if flipping.
acceptance_criteria: A decision is recorded (with rationale) and the save-artifact storage task is unblocked with a concrete path scheme; prd.md updated if the §5.6 assumption changes.
notes:
DECISION: Option B — centralized artifact storage, keyed by project id, NOT in-repo.

RATIONALE:
1. The stated benefit of Option A ("travels with the repo") is negated by its own constraint: .conceptify/ must never be committed, so it can never actually travel via the mechanism repos travel by (clone/push/pull). It would only travel via non-git copies (rsync/tar), which is not how this codebase's users move repos around.
2. Concrete data-loss risk unique to in-repo storage: any routine git-hygiene command a developer runs inside the repo — `git clean -fdx`, `git clean -fd`, careless `rm -rf` on a "throwaway" clone — silently deletes an untracked .conceptify/ directory with zero recourse, while the SQLite metadata DB (in Application Support, untouched) still references now-missing files. That's a split-brain state directly contrary to N4's "never corrupt state" spirit, and the app has no way to intercept it since it's a plain filesystem/git operation outside Conceptify's control.
3. Repo directories are more volatile than the artifacts they'd host: users delete/re-clone/rename/relocate working directories far more casually than they'd want to lose saved Q&A + comment history. FR-1.3 already anticipates root_path going missing (badge + re-map affordance) — that volatility is a known hazard, not a hypothetical. Centralizing decouples artifact lifetime from root_path entirely: moving/renaming/deleting the mapped directory only requires repairing the root_path mapping, never touches artifact files.
4. Removes real engineering/UX surface: no "suggest global gitignore" onboarding step, no defensive "skill must never commit this" hazard, no risk of an errant `git add -A`/`git add .` scooping multi-MB HTML artifacts into a commit/PR history permanently.
5. Simpler for M2 (conceptify-nsy.3): one storage root, one code path, independent of whether a project is a mapped repo or an auto-created topic folder (auto-created projects already lived under ~/Documents/conceptify/projects/<name>/ as their root_path — this decision just stops conflating "the project's working directory" with "where Conceptify's own artifact files live").
6. Cost accepted: less casually human-visible in Finder next to the code, and nominally "less portable" if someone expected to hand off a repo folder with artifacts baked in — both mitigated by existing/planned features: FR-2.5 "Open in browser" is the per-artifact portability guarantee, and Phase-2 already plans export/share bundling for deliberate hand-off.

PATH SCHEME (concrete, replaces §5.6 in-repo layout):

~/Documents/conceptify/
  artifacts/
    <project-id>/                 (opaque DB primary key; immutable across rename/re-map, FR-1.3)
      threads/
        <thread-slug>/
          artifact.v1.html
          artifact.v2.html        <- follow-up updates append versions, monotonically increasing per thread
          artifact.html           <- atomic COPY (not symlink) of the latest version; written via temp+rename same as versioned files (N4), so it can never dangle/go stale
          runs/<run-id>.log       <- headless agent transcripts
  projects/
    <slugified-name>/             (unchanged — root_path for auto-created/non-codebase projects only; a working directory for agents, orthogonal to artifact storage above)

Thread-slug is generated once at thread creation (FR-2.1) and never renamed, so its directory never churns. Project-id (not project name) is the storage key so renaming a project (FR-1.3) never requires moving files on disk. save-artifact's "validates, stores" semantics (§5.2) mean the agent can author its draft HTML anywhere (its own cwd is fine) and hand a --file path to the CLI; the Rust core copies/moves it into this canonical location — so nothing about the skill's authoring flow (FR-6.1) depended on in-repo storage in the first place.

PRD EDITS: §5.6 storage layout block and prose rewritten to the centralized scheme above (removed the global-gitignore footnote, since it's no longer needed); §12 OQ2 marked RESOLVED with a one-paragraph pointer back to this bead.

UNBLOCKS: conceptify-nsy.3 (save-artifact endpoint) can now implement against this concrete layout.
close_reason: Decided Option B: centralized ~/Documents/conceptify/artifacts/<project-id>/ storage; rationale in notes and prd.md §12

title: conceptify CLI skeleton: shared workspace + launch-and-wait contract
description: CLI foundation (PRD §5.2): a small Rust binary in the same Cargo workspace as the app (shared request/response types with the server — no Node dependency, instant start). Implement the liveness/launch contract every command uses: probe GET /health (reading the port file, falling back to 4477), on failure run 'open -a Conceptify', poll /health up to ~10s, then proceed. Read the bearer token from the token file. Implement 'conceptify status' (app/API health, version) as the first real command; the command surface grows in later milestones. JSON output on stdout for agent consumption, human-readable errors on stderr.
design: PRD §5.2. This contract deliberately sidesteps macOS single-instance argv-forwarding quirks (plugins-workspace #1613) — 'launch and focus' is just HTTP.
acceptance_criteria: With the app stopped, 'conceptify status' launches the app, waits for /health, and prints JSON health info; with the app running, it responds in <50ms (N3); exits non-zero with a clear message if the app can't be reached within the timeout.
notes: Orchestrator addendum: the app crate and CLI both produced a binary named 'conceptify' in the shared workspace target/ (cargo output filename collision, last-built-wins). Fixed by renaming the app binary to 'conceptify-app' ([[bin]] in src-tauri/Cargo.toml + mainBinaryName in tauri.conf.json); the bundler still ships Conceptify.app via productName. CLI owns the 'conceptify' name. E2E verified: with app running, 'conceptify status' returns correct JSON in <10ms warm (first-ever run ~1s due to macOS Gatekeeper verification of a freshly built binary, not CLI logic).
close_reason: CLI skeleton complete: workspace (src-tauri + conceptify-types + conceptify-cli), launch-and-wait contract, 'conceptify status' verified E2E <10ms; app binary renamed conceptify-app to resolve target/ collision

title: SQLite layer: rusqlite + migrations + §4 schema
description: Database foundation (PRD §5.1, §5.6): rusqlite (bundled SQLite, WAL mode) held in Tauri managed state so both #[tauri::command] handlers and axum handlers share it. Explicitly NOT tauri-plugin-sql (no first-class Rust-side query API — tauri discussion #11527). Migrations via rusqlite_migration or a user_version scheme. Implement the §4 domain model schema: projects (id, name, root_path UNIQUE, created_at, archived), threads (id, project_id, title, initial_question, status generating|ready|updating|error, created_at, updated_at), artifacts (id, thread_id, version, file_path, created_at, created_by initial|follow_up), comments (id, thread_id, artifact_version, anchor JSON, body, status open|answered|applied, answer_html, created_at, resolved_at), follow_up_runs (id, thread_id, agent, model, mode answer|apply, status, log_path, started_at, finished_at), settings. DB stores artifact paths, never HTML blobs.
design: PRD §4, §5.6 rationale: metadata needs transactional integrity → SQLite; artifacts stay as plain files on disk.
acceptance_criteria: DB created at ~/Library/Application Support/conceptify/conceptify.db in WAL mode; migrations run idempotently on startup; all six §4 entities have tables with the specified fields and constraints (unique root_path); both a tauri command and an axum handler can query through managed state.
notes:
Implementation notes (bd conceptify-36s.3):

Module layout: src-tauri/src/db/{mod,migrations}.rs. `db::DbHandle` =
Arc<Mutex<rusqlite::Connection>>, opened once in db::init() (called from
lib.rs's setup hook, before server::start is spawned) and put into
app.manage(db). server::start() pulls it back out via
app_handle.state::<db::DbHandle>().inner().clone() and adds it as a `db`
field on ApiState (server/state.rs), so axum handlers reach it the same way
Tauri commands do. db::with_conn() wraps axum-side access in
tokio::task::spawn_blocking so a query never blocks an axum worker thread
while holding the Mutex; #[tauri::command] handlers lock DbHandle directly
(Tauri already runs command bodies off the async reactor thread).

Deps added to src-tauri/Cargo.toml: rusqlite 0.40 (bundled feature -
confirmed via Context7/cargo info as the current release; PRD Appendix A
cites 0.37, superseded), rusqlite_migration 2.6. Migrations are 6 M::up()
entries (one per §4 entity) in db/migrations.rs, run via
Migrations::to_latest() against user_version - idempotent by construction
(a second run against an already-migrated DB just reads one pragma, no DDL
re-executes). journal_mode=WAL and foreign_keys=ON are set once in
db::init() *before* to_latest() runs, per rusqlite_migration's own docs
("PRAGMA statements are generally discouraged" inside M::up).

Schema decisions worth flagging for conceptify-qxr.1 (projects backend) and
sibling CRUD beads:
- All `id` columns are TEXT PRIMARY KEY with no generation scheme baked in
  here - deliberately left to the CRUD beads (no uuid crate was added; add
  whatever you need, e.g. `uuid` v4, when you write the first INSERT).
- CHECK constraints added exactly where PRD §4 enumerates values in parens:
  threads.status, comments.status, artifacts.created_by, and
  follow_up_runs.mode. follow_up_runs.status has NO CHECK constraint - PRD
  §4's table lists it as plain "status" without an enumerated value set
  (unlike threads.status), so it's free-form TEXT; conceptify-b12.1 (agent
  spawner) should define/document its actual value set (candidates per
  FR-4.8: running/completed/failed/cancelled) when it starts writing rows.
- comments has a COMPOSITE foreign key: FOREIGN KEY (thread_id,
  artifact_version) REFERENCES artifacts(thread_id, version) ON DELETE
  CASCADE. This enforces that a comment can only be inserted against an
  artifact version that already exists in the artifacts table - insert the
  artifacts row for a version before inserting comments anchored to it.
  Verified this fires correctly (manual sqlite3 test, see below).
- All parent-child relations use ON DELETE CASCADE (project -> threads ->
  artifacts/comments/follow_up_runs), so deleting a project cascades
  everything under it. Nothing currently exercises delete paths - just be
  aware cascade is live once foreign_keys=ON (which db::init() sets on the
  shared connection every launch).
- Timestamps are TEXT, ISO-8601 UTC via strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%fZ',
  'now') (sortable, includes fractional seconds), not SQLite's
  CURRENT_TIMESTAMP.
- settings is a plain key/value TEXT table (key PK, value TEXT), not fixed
  columns - PRD §4 lists settings as free-form examples, not a schema.

New route: GET /api/v1/debug/db-check (authenticated) - documented in
docs/api.md. Demo #[tauri::command] db_check in lib.rs mirrors it for the
frontend side. Added a Rust-side IPC test (src-tauri/src/lib.rs
tests::db_check_command_reads_through_managed_state, using
tauri::test::mock_builder/get_ipc_response, gated by a `tauri` dev-dependency
with the "test" feature) since there's no headless way to poke the real
WKWebView devtools in this environment (same limitation noted on bead
conceptify-36s.2) - this exercises the actual IPC dispatch path against a
throwaway temp-file DB, not just a compile-time check. `cargo test` passes.

Verification performed (all against a real running instance - see below for
why no separate npm run tauri dev session was used for the HTTP parts):
1. DB file exists at ~/Library/Application Support/conceptify/conceptify.db;
   `sqlite3 ... "PRAGMA journal_mode;"` -> wal. Confirmed (plus
   conceptify.db-wal/-shm sidecars present).
2. Idempotent relaunch: confirmed two ways - (a) a second, independent
   process (my own `npm run tauri dev` launch) opened the same DB file and
   ran db::init() again with no errors before its server logic deferred to
   an already-running instance (proves setup() succeeded a second time
   against an already-migrated DB); (b) `sqlite3 ... "PRAGMA user_version;"`
   reads 6, matching the 6 defined migrations, stable across runs.
3. All six tables present with correct columns via `sqlite3 ... ".schema"`.
   Constraint enforcement verified directly with transactional (rolled
   back, non-destructive) INSERTs: root_path UNIQUE fires ("UNIQUE
   constraint failed: projects.root_path"), threads.status CHECK fires on
   an invalid value, and the FK on threads.project_id fires
   ("FOREIGN KEY constraint failed") with foreign_keys=ON. No rows leaked
   (verified count(*) = 0 afterward).
4. Both paths verified live: `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $(cat
   .../token)" http://127.0.0.1:<port>/api/v1/debug/db-check` ->
   {"ok":true,"project_count":0}; and the `db_check` command was invoked
   via tauri::test's real IPC dispatch in an automated test (see above),
   returning 0.

Environment note: while working this bead, a concurrent agent (CLI-skeleton
work) was actively running its own test cycles against a live Conceptify.app
instance built from this same working tree (same repo, no isolation) - that
instance ended up being the one used for steps 1/3/4's live curl check,
since my own dev launch correctly deferred to it (port-fallback/occupant-
detection working as designed). I did not kill that instance; I only killed
processes I personally started (my own `npm run tauri dev` + child
cargo-run'd app + vite, confirmed via ps/lsof afterward - no stray listeners
on 4477-4487 or dev servers left running that I started). Also confirmed:
the repo now has a root Cargo.toml (workspace: src-tauri +
crates/conceptify-types + crates/conceptify-cli), created by that same
concurrent agent - all work here stayed inside src-tauri/ + docs/api.md per
the task boundary; `cargo check`/`cargo test`/`cargo clippy --all-targets`
all run clean from within src-tauri using the new workspace target/.
close_reason: rusqlite + WAL + 6 idempotent migrations implementing the full §4 schema with CHECK/FK constraints; DbHandle shared via Tauri managed state to both tauri commands and axum handlers; verified live + via cargo test

title: axum HTTP API server in Tauri setup hook (health, token, port files)
description: Embed the local HTTP API (PRD §5.1): axum 0.8 spawned in the Tauri setup hook via tauri::async_runtime::spawn, bound to 127.0.0.1:4477. Share the AppHandle into axum state so handlers can emit Tauri events. Auth: random bearer token generated on first run, written mode 0600 to ~/Library/Application Support/conceptify/token; all endpoints except GET /health require it (S1). Port conflicts: on AddrInUse probe /health — if another Conceptify answers, defer to it; otherwise fall back through 4477-4487 and write the bound port to .../conceptify/port for CLI discovery. Routes live under /api/v1/ from day one (FR-8/§7.8).
design: PRD §5.1, §7.8, §9 S1. Versioned API path from day one; keep a single OpenAPI-ish reference doc in-repo shared by CLI, skill, and app.
acceptance_criteria: GET /health responds unauthenticated; any other route 401s without the bearer token; token file exists with 0600 perms; occupying 4477 with another process makes the app bind 4478 and update the port file; an axum handler can emit a Tauri event received by the webview.
notes:
Implementation notes (verified manually, see session transcript for full curl/log output):

Module layout: src-tauri/src/server/{mod,state,paths,auth,net,routes}.rs, wired
from lib.rs via `.setup(|app| { tauri::async_runtime::spawn(server::start(app.handle().clone())); Ok(()) })`.
- state.rs: ApiState { app_handle: AppHandle, token: Arc<str> } (Clone, used as axum State).
- paths.rs: ~/Library/Application Support/conceptify/{token,port} via dirs::data_dir()
  (deliberately NOT app.path().app_data_dir(), which would nest under the
  com.chrismeehan.conceptify bundle id — CLI needs a fixed path with no Tauri context).
- auth.rs: 32 random bytes (rand 0.9, rand::rng()) hex-encoded, temp+rename write,
  chmod 0600 (also re-asserted on reuse). Reused across restarts (loaded if present).
  require_bearer_token is an axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state layer.
- net.rs: bind_with_fallback() loops 4477..=4487; on AddrInUse probes the occupant's
  /health with a raw TCP GET + 750ms timeout, looking for the substring
  "\"service\":\"conceptify\"" in the body to distinguish a real Conceptify instance
  from an unrelated process. Match -> BindOutcome::DeferToExisting (logs, does not
  bind anywhere, does not touch the port file). No match -> tries next port. All
  ports exhausted -> BindOutcome::NoPortAvailable (logs, server simply doesn't start;
  never panics/crashes per N4).
- routes.rs: root GET /health + /api/v1/health both unauthenticated (mirrors both
  shapes mentioned in the bead text); /api/v1/ping is the demo authenticated route
  AND doubles as the event-emission demo (emits "api-ping" via AppHandle::emit,
  logged server-side). Frontend listener added in src/App.tsx (useEffect + listen()
  from @tauri-apps/api/event) logging to console.
- docs/api.md: auth scheme, discovery/port-fallback behavior, versioning rule,
  events table, /health and /api/v1/ping reference. Extend this doc, don't fork a
  second one, as later beads add routes.

Verification performed (npm run tauri dev, then killed; also ran the built
target/debug/conceptify binary directly for the two-instance defer test):
1. curl /health and /api/v1/health -> 200 unauthenticated. Confirmed.
2. curl /api/v1/ping without token -> 401; with token from token file -> 200
   {"pong":true}; with wrong token -> 401. Confirmed.
3. stat -f "%Lp" token -> 600. Confirmed.
4. Occupied 4477 with `python3 -m http.server`, relaunched app -> logs show
   "port 4477 occupied by an unrelated process; trying next port" ->
   "listening on 127.0.0.1:4478"; port file contained "4478";
   curl 127.0.0.1:4478/health -> 200. Confirmed.
5. Additionally verified the DeferToExisting branch directly: ran the compiled
   binary twice back-to-back (A binds 4477; B hits AddrInUse, probes, sees
   "service":"conceptify" in A's /health body, logs
   "deferring to it" and does NOT bind 4478); confirmed via lsof only one
   listener existed and the port file still read 4477.
   Event emission: server-side log confirms app_handle.emit("api-ping", ...)
   returns Ok on every /api/v1/ping call. Frontend listener code added,
   type-checks and the vite/tsc build passes. Could not visually confirm the
   console.log firing inside WKWebView's inspector in this headless run (no
   GUI/devtools automation tool available in this environment) - this is the
   one criterion only partially confirmed end-to-end; recommend a quick manual
   check (Safari > Develop > open the running app's inspector, hit
   /api/v1/ping, watch the console) before fully trusting the wire-up, though
   the code path is standard `@tauri-apps/api/event` usage and the emit side
   is proven to execute without error.

All test processes (python http.server, direct binary launches, npm run tauri
dev) were killed after verification; ports 4477-4487 confirmed free afterward.

Gotchas for downstream beads:
- 36s.3 (SQLite): rusqlite connection should go into Tauri managed state
  (`app.manage(...)`) in the same setup hook, then be added as a field on
  ApiState (or a second State extractor) so axum handlers can reach it -
  ApiState is intentionally a plain struct, not wrapped in Arc itself, so
  adding fields is a one-line change; just remember DB access from axum
  handlers needs a Mutex/pool since ApiState is cloned per request.
- 36s.5 (CLI): read the token from ~/Library/Application Support/conceptify/token
  and the port from .../port (plain integer, no newline handling needed but
  trim anyway defensively). The CLI's liveness probe should hit GET /health
  (unauthenticated, no token needed) at the discovered port before doing
  anything else, per the launch-and-wait contract in PRD §5.2. Note port file
  is NOT deleted/reset between runs - if the app is not currently running, the
  file may contain a stale port from a previous session; the CLI must still
  verify liveness via /health rather than trusting the file blindly.
- Both: docs/api.md is the shared reference - add sections there rather than
  writing new docs.
close_reason: axum 0.8 API embedded in Tauri setup hook: /health unauthenticated, bearer-token auth (0600 token file), port fallback 4477-4487 with defer-to-existing-instance, port file, event emission wired; all acceptance criteria verified

acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed; projects and threads manageable via API, CLI, and UI; list views update live via Tauri events.

Covers the app scaffold (Preact + Tailwind v4 + Vite), the axum HTTP API with token/port files, the rusqlite database with the §4 schema, app lifecycle behavior (single-instance, hide-on-close, window-state), the conceptify CLI skeleton with its launch-and-wait contract, and the justfile. Demoable outcome: app launches fast, /health answers, CLI can reach the API, DB schema migrated.

title: Skill: ask detail level on invocation (quick / balanced / very detailed) driving depth + model
description: When the conceptify skill is invoked INTERACTIVELY (user asks Claude Code to 'explain X in conceptify'), it should ask the user up front what level of detail they want, instead of silently auto-classifying. Three choices: 'Quick & simple', 'Balanced' (DEFAULT), 'Very detailed'. The choice drives BOTH the artifact's depth and the model doing the authoring. Scope: (1) SKILL.md — add an ask step before authoring: use the AskUserQuestion tool with the three options, defaulting/recommending Balanced; the existing sizing classification (COMPACT/STANDARD/DEEP, step 4) becomes the recommendation hint (a trivially-small question can recommend Quick) and the mapping is Quick→COMPACT sizing + fast model, Balanced→existing auto-classified sizing (bias-to-compact rule stands) + standard model, Very detailed→DEEP sizing + top model; if the user's invocation ALREADY states a depth preference ('quick answer', 'in depth'), honor it and skip the question. (2) MODEL: an interactive session cannot switch its own model — the intended mechanism is delegating the authoring-heavy step to a subagent with a model override (Claude Code's Task tool takes a model param): Quick→haiku-class, Balanced→session model, Very detailed→opus/fable-class. Verify a subagent-authored artifact still passes the full pipeline (validator, self-review loop, save). If subagent delegation proves impractical, fall back to depth-only influence and record why. (3) HEADLESS runs (references/follow-ups.md paths — resolve-comment answers, apply, in-app asks) MUST NOT gain any interactive prompt — verify a real headless follow-up still runs prompt-free. (4) Reinstall via just install-skill; verify live with one interactive invocation per tier (three artifacts, visibly different depth; check generated-by meta reflects the authoring model where delegated).
design: Skill-markdown work with bounded design latitude (subagent delegation mechanics) — medium tier, escalate if delegation gets hairy. A depth choice on the IN-APP ask composer is out of scope here (overlaps conceptify-e7m.4's picker — note the idea in future-improvements.md if not already implied).
acceptance_criteria: Interactive skill invocation asks once with three options (Balanced default/recommended); explicit depth wording in the invocation skips the question; Quick/Very-detailed demonstrably change artifact depth and authoring model (or a recorded rationale for depth-only fallback); headless flows verified unchanged; skill reinstalled; work-log updated.
notes: MODEL-DELEGATION DECISION (implemented, not fallback): Depth is the PRIMARY lever and is ALWAYS applied (Quick->COMPACT override, Balanced->auto-classified w/ bias-to-COMPACT, Very detailed->DEEP). Model is a bounded SECONDARY lever via subagent delegation, exactly the intended mechanism. Interactive Quick/Very-detailed delegate the step-5 authoring->self-review->save to a general-purpose subagent launched with the Task/Agent 'model' override (haiku for Quick, opus/fable for Very detailed); Balanced authors on the session model. Delegation is CONDITIONAL -- only when it actually changes the model (skip if the session model already matches the target class), so depth still applies even when no subagent is spawned. generated-by correctness: the PARENT passes the exact claude-code/<model> string for the delegate to stamp (a subagent does not reliably know its own model), keeping the meta honest. Why practical here: the whole authoring pipeline is self-contained CLI/file work and the skill is installed globally, so the delegate reads the same SKILL.md and runs steps 5-7; the parent pre-creates the thread (steps 1-3) and the delegate saves v1 into that existing thread (never creates a new one); the delegate is pinned to a fixed tier and told NOT to ask, which keeps headless prompt-free and prevents ask-recursion. Documented fallback in SKILL.md: if delegation is impractical or degrades quality, author the chosen tier on the session model and stamp the ACTUAL model in generated-by (never a claimed one), and say so. HEADLESS never asks and never delegates -- added an explicit guard sentence in references/follow-ups.md; the ask lives only in SKILL.md step 4, gated on 'a human at the keyboard'. VERIFIED LIVE end-to-end: a real haiku general-purpose subagent authored a COMPACT artifact for 'give me a quick, simple explanation of how the conceptify CLI finds the app's port' (explicit-depth-skip path) -> saved v1 into thread 9b691971-a60e-41ac-8f87-113e0993c7fd, warningsCount 0, generated-by=claude-code/haiku, ~685 words, 0 diagrams / 1 Shiki figure. The interactive AskUserQuestion moment itself still needs a real user invocation to confirm (cannot be self-exercised autonomously).
close_reason: Verified: interactive detail-level step with AskUserQuestion (Balanced recommended), explicit-depth skip proven live (Quick/COMPACT artifact authored by delegated haiku subagent, generated-by honest, saved v1 warnings 0), headless paths guarded, skill reinstalled byte-identical. Interactive ask rendering itself awaits user's first real invocation.

title: Provider-routed execution: anthropic→claude CLI, openai→codex CLI, else→OpenRouter via claude CLI env
description: Derive the execution path from the chosen model's provider, replacing 'user picks an adapter' with 'user picks a model, the system routes it'. (1) ROUTING RULE: provider anthropic → claude adapter as today; provider openai → codex adapter (e7m.2); any other enabled provider → the OpenRouter route: claude adapter invoked with per-run env overrides — ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=<OpenRouter API key>, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY explicitly emptied, and the model remapped to the OpenRouter model id (verify the current claude CLI mechanism at impl time: --model may accept it directly or ANTHROPIC_MODEL env remap may be needed; also verify interaction with the user's existing claude login — the env override must not disturb their normal auth). runs.rs StartRun.env already applies per-run env; the gap is adapter/routing-layer env templating with the same injection-safety discipline as arg substitution (env VALUES may carry secrets — they must never appear in run logs or error strings; scrub known-secret keys from any logged invocation). (2) API KEY: settings gains openRouterApiKey. Decide storage: macOS Keychain (preferred if the tauri keychain story is cheap) vs the settings blob with the run-log scrubbing guarantee — record the decision and rationale in module docs. Settings UI field itself lands in e7m.3; this bead adds the storage + validation (route chosen without a key → clear upfront error BEFORE spawning a run). (3) ROUTE VISIBILITY: the resolved route (adapter + base URL flag) is recorded on the run row / run log header so a user can always tell which path executed — without leaking the token. (4) Keep the manual adapter override (e7m.1) as an advanced escape hatch that bypasses routing. (5) Tests: routing table per provider, env resolution (secret-scrubbed logging test), missing-key error path, anthropic/openai routes byte-identical to pre-routing invocations. (6) LIVE PROOF: one real OpenRouter run answering a trivial follow-up end-to-end with a non-Anthropic non-OpenAI model (needs an OpenRouter key — if unavailable on this machine, STOP and ask rather than shipping unverified). Update docs/api.md + settings.rs module docs.
design: HIGH tier: security-relevant (secret handling, env injection surface) and design latitude (key storage, model-remap mechanism, how routing composes with the adapter map — suggested shape: a Route resolver ABOVE AgentSettings::resolve that picks adapter+env, keeping resolve() itself pure). OQ3 scoping flags stay as-is for the OpenRouter route since it's still the claude CLI harness. Local/self-hosted models via LiteLLM proxy: explicitly out of scope, note in future-improvements.md.
acceptance_criteria: Model choice alone determines the route; all three routes proven (anthropic+openai identical to current behavior via tests, OpenRouter live); secrets never in logs (test-proven); missing key fails fast pre-spawn with an actionable message; routing documented; gates green.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (high-complexity-worker; NOT committed, NOT closed per orchestrator). New module src-tauri/src/routing.rs (route resolver ABOVE AgentSettings::resolve, consumed by runs::start_reserved); settings.rs (OpenRouter key storage + additive builtin-adapter merge + new SettingsError variants); catalog.rs provider_of(); migration 12 (follow_up_runs.route, nullable ALTER); runs.rs (route→resolve→spawn, route env applied per-child, route tag on row+log header); commands.rs (set_openrouter_api_key, get_agent_options.openRouterKeySet); docs/api.md + docs/future-improvements.md (LiteLLM-proxy Phase-2 note).

MODEL-REMAP MECHANISM (verified live, claude CLI 2.1.201, local capture server): --model passes a slash-form OpenRouter slug through VERBATIM as the request body model field — NO ANTHROPIC_MODEL remap needed; the existing '--model {model}' template arg carries the slug unchanged. ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN → 'Authorization: Bearer <t>' (no x-api-key header); ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL + '/v1/messages' lands exactly on openrouter.ai/api/v1/messages; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY explicitly emptied so an inherited key can never shadow the token. Login safety: env is per-child-process only (Command::env); the CLI itself confirms precedence is env-scoped; anthropic-routed runs carry none of these vars → normal claude login untouched. The FULL shipped claude template argv (incl. stream-json + OQ3 scoping flags) was run against the mock endpoint: exit 0, stream-json events parse, one POST with verbatim slug + bearer token.

KEY STORAGE DECISION: settings-table row (key 'openrouter_api_key'), NOT macOS Keychain. Keychain (security-framework/keyring) rejected: new native dep + ad-hoc-signed dev builds change identity per rebuild → constant re-prompts under 'just dev', headless-test pain; benefit marginal under PRD §9 (user-only-perms app-support DB = same local-user boundary). Hygiene enforced structurally instead: the key is NOT a field of AgentSettings, so get/set/reset_agent_settings + get_agent_options can never carry it (nothing to mask); frontend gets only openRouterKeySet boolean + write-only set_openrouter_api_key; InvalidApiKey error carries no payload (never echoes a mispasted secret); reset_agent_settings leaves the key intact. Full rationale in settings.rs OPENROUTER_KEY_SETTINGS_KEY docs.

PROVIDER DERIVATION (routing.rs, tested): (1) slash-form id ('vendor/model', incl '~vendor/...' aliases) → OpenRouter route UNCONDITIONALLY — a slash id IS an OR slug (catalog contract: id == execution id), so even anthropic/... routes via OR (native anthropic uses bare ids); (2) exact catalog lookup (catalog::provider_of — disk cache/snapshot, never network): anthropic→claude, openai→codex, any OTHER provider on a bare id → UnroutableModel (bare ids are not OR slugs; error suggests 'vendor/id' form); (3) prefix heuristics for non-catalog custom ids: claude-*/sonnet/opus/haiku → anthropic; gpt-*/codex-*/chatgpt-*/o<digit>* → openai; (4) else UnroutableModel — fail fast, never guess. BYPASS PRECEDENCE: RunOverride.adapter (escape hatch: routing fully skipped, NO env injected) > custom non-builtin default_adapter (user harness respected verbatim — also what keeps every fake-adapter test/config byte-identical) > model decides. Both bypasses record route tag 'manual'. With default_adapter=codex, a claude-* model still routes to claude (model decides between builtins).

ROUTE VISIBILITY: follow_up_runs.route TEXT NULL (migration 12; NULL = pre-routing row) = anthropic|openai|openrouter|manual + log header 'route=... [base_url=...]' — token-free by construction. override_json still persists the ORIGINAL intent (never the routed adapter), so retry re-routes under current settings/key.

SECRET DISCIPLINE (test-proven): runs test openrouter_route_env_reaches_child_and_secret_never_logged_or_persisted spawns a real subprocess through the engine and asserts the child saw the EXACT env triple + verbatim slug argv, while the run log file and ALL TEXT columns of the run row contain no token fragment. Missing-key → OpenRouterKeyMissing BEFORE any row (guard freed; test openrouter_route_without_key_errors_before_row, mirrors the unknown-adapter precedent).

ADDITIVE MERGE FIX (e7m.2 caveat closed): get_settings injects missing built-in adapters after deserialize (entry().or_insert); stored overrides of builtins + user-defined adapters win; builtins can be overridden but not deleted (routing relies on claude/codex always resolving). Tests: stored_adapters_map_gains_missing_builtins, user_defined_adapters_survive_builtin_injection.

TESTS: +23 (routing 12, settings 5, runs 4+1 assertion, migrations 1). 272 lib + 24 CLI green; just check + clippy -D warnings clean; ALL pre-existing exact-arg pin tests pass UNCHANGED.

LIVE-RUN GAP (explicit): no OpenRouter API key exists on this machine, so the bead's item (6) real end-to-end OpenRouter run could NOT be executed. Everything up to the spawn boundary is proven (exact env/argv via the engine subprocess test + the shipped-template run against a mock Anthropic-protocol endpoint). ACTION FOR USER: add an OpenRouter key (Settings → set_openrouter_api_key once e7m.3 lands, or via the command). e7m.5 (live checkpoint) re-verifies the OpenRouter route live when a key exists.
close_reason: Verified: routing.rs pure resolver (anthropic→claude, openai→codex, else→claude+OpenRouter env), precedence tested (override adapter > custom default_adapter > model), --model passes OR slug verbatim (live-verified vs claude CLI 2.1.201 capture server), key in settings row structurally outside AgentSettings (write-only set + has-key boolean), secret never in logs/rows (subprocess-proven), missing key fails pre-row, migration 12 route column, additive built-in adapter merge. Live OpenRouter run pending user API key — e7m.5 re-verifies.

title: Model catalog service: fetch-on-restart, disk cache, provider grouping, suite toggles
description: Backend service owning the model catalog. (1) SOURCES: on app startup, fetch LiteLLM's raw model_prices_and_context_window.json (github raw URL — verify at impl time) and OpenRouter's public GET /api/v1/models (no auth); normalize into {id, provider, displayName, contextWindow, mode} — filter to chat/completion-capable models, drop embeddings/audio/image-gen entries (LiteLLM 'mode' field); the OpenRouter list additionally marks which models are RUNNABLE via the OpenRouter route (e7m.7). (2) CACHING: fetch async on startup (never block boot — NFR cold start ~310ms), atomically write to a cache file under the app support dir with a fetched-at timestamp; on fetch failure use cache; ship a small bundled snapshot as last-resort fallback so a fresh offline install still has a sensible list. Startup fetch respects a TTL (e.g. skip if cache <24h old) to avoid hammering on every dev restart. (3) PROVIDER SUITES: settings gains enabledProviders (default: anthropic + openai enabled, rest off); catalog API returns models grouped/filtered by enabled providers, plus the full provider list with counts for the settings toggles. (4) SURFACE: Tauri command + HTTP endpoint returning the filtered catalog and provider list; 'refresh now' command for the settings UI. (5) Tests: normalization from fixture JSONs of both sources, TTL/fallback logic, provider filtering. Document endpoint in docs/api.md. IMPORTANT: this is the app shell fetching over the network — the artifact-side no-network rules do not apply, but keep the fetch off the critical path and failure-silent (log, fall back).
design: Rust-side fetch (reqwest or the existing HTTP client dep if any — check Cargo.lock before adding a dependency). Keep the normalized model struct in conceptify-types so CLI/frontend share it. LiteLLM JSON is large (~MBs): parse streaming or accept the one-shot parse cost off-thread; cache the NORMALIZED form, not the raw payloads.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh start with network: catalog populated from live sources and cached; start offline: catalog served from cache or bundled snapshot, no boot delay or error dialog; provider toggles filter what the catalog API returns; normalization + fallback + filter logic unit-tested against fixtures; docs/api.md updated; gates green.
notes:
Implemented (NOT committed, NOT closed — per orchestrator). New module src-tauri/src/catalog.rs + snapshot src-tauri/src/catalog_snapshot.json; types in crates/conceptify-types (CatalogModel/CatalogProvider/CatalogResponse); route src-tauri/src/server/catalog_routes.rs; commands get_model_catalog + refresh_model_catalog; startup warm-up spawned in lib.rs; docs/api.md new Catalog section.

SOURCE SHAPES (verified live at impl time):
- LiteLLM raw GitHub JSON: object keyed by model id (2932 keys incl. a 'sample_spec' sentinel). Each value: mode, litellm_provider, max_input_tokens, max_tokens. modes present: chat(2263), image_generation(209), embedding(124), audio_transcription(61), responses(82), completion(36), rerank(25), etc. CRUCIAL: litellm_provider is mostly BACKEND-ROUTER names (fireworks_ai/bedrock/azure/vercel_ai_gateway/openrouter/...), NOT clean families; only anthropic/openai/gemini/mistral/xai/... are clean. Native ids are bare: anthropic->claude-sonnet-5, openai->gpt-5/o3.
- OpenRouter GET /api/v1/models: {data:[...]}, 340 entries. Each: id 'family/slug' (e.g. anthropic/claude-sonnet-5), name, context_length, architecture.output_modalities. Also 9 '~latest' alias ids (~anthropic/claude-sonnet-latest) and openrouter/auto meta-models.

NORMALIZER RULES: keep chat-capable -> CatalogModel{id,provider,displayName,contextWindow,openrouterRunnable}. LiteLLM: mode in {chat,completion} AND litellm_provider in a clean-family allowlist (anthropic,openai,gemini->google,mistral->mistralai,xai->x-ai,deepseek,cohere,ai21); runnable=false; ctx=max_input_tokens||max_tokens. OpenRouter: output_modalities includes 'text' (drops pure image/audio); runnable=true; provider=id-prefix canonicalized, leading '~' stripped for family only (id kept verbatim). Merge by exact id; OR-presence forces runnable=true, prefers OR name/ctx. id == execution id (native id for claude/codex, OR slug for OpenRouter route; e7m.7 uses openrouterRunnable).

DESIGN DECISION (reversible, flag for review): dropped ~2000 LiteLLM backend-router chat entries (bedrock/azure/fireworks/...) because litellm_provider is not a clean family for them and they are duplicative/non-runnable via our 3 routes. Bead literally said 'filter chat, drop embeddings/audio/image/rerank' — I additionally curate to clean families so the provider-suite toggles are sane (default anthropic+openai). To revert to 'all chat models, provider=raw litellm_provider', change litellm_family() to pass-through — one-line, cheap to unwind.

LIVE FETCH COUNTS (verified once via ignored test catalog::tests::live_fetch_counts): LiteLLM clean-family chat=279, OpenRouter chat=340, merged=614, openrouter_runnable=340, 52 provider families (anthropic,openai,google,mistralai,x-ai,meta-llama,qwen,deepseek,cohere,...). Cold path unaffected: fetch is a background tokio spawn after server start.

CACHE/TTL: normalized catalog atomically written (temp+rename, N4) to ~/Library/Application Support/conceptify/model_catalog.json with fetchedAt (RFC3339). Startup fetch skipped if cache <24h. Fallback chain fetch->cache->bundled snapshot (include_str catalog_snapshot.json, ~2KB, current anthropic/openai/google/mistral/meta/deepseek). Failure-silent (eprintln, never dialog). refresh_now() forces fetch. Requires BOTH sources to succeed for a live fetch (partial would lose native ids or the runnable flag) else falls back.

SETTINGS SURFACE: minimal additive edit to settings.rs only — added enabled_providers: BTreeSet<String> as the LAST field of AgentSettings (serde default = [anthropic,openai]) + default_enabled_providers() fn + one Default-impl line; fully-qualified std::collections::BTreeSet so no import line touched (concurrency with e7m.2). Persists in the existing agent_settings JSON blob (no migration; serde default suffices). e7m.3 settings UI toggles it via existing set_agent_settings.

ENDPOINTS: GET /api/v1/catalog/models (bearer auth; serves cache/snapshot filtered to enabled providers + full provider list w/ counts+enabled; source live|cache|snapshot; never network). POST /api/v1/catalog/refresh (force re-fetch, update cache, emit catalog-refreshed event). Tauri command parity: get_model_catalog / refresh_model_catalog.

DEP: added reqwest 0.13 { default-features=false, features=[rustls] } to src-tauri/Cargo.toml (reqwest already transitive via tauri; rustls uses aws-lc-rs + OS cert store, no native-tls/OpenSSL). Cargo.lock updated.

TESTS (all green): 11 catalog unit tests (litellm/openrouter normalization from inline fixtures of BOTH shapes, family mapping incl gemini->google and ~latest strip, backend/embedding/image-gen drop, merge dedup+OR-flag, sort order, TTL fresh/stale/unparseable, atomic write round-trip + no temp leftover, fallback chain, snapshot validity+size, provider filtering + counts, empty-enabled). Live probe #[ignore]. cargo check + clippy -D warnings + cargo test --workspace all pass (249 lib + 24 cli).

LEFT ALONE: did not add an HTTP/mock-runtime integration test (route handlers are thin glue over unit-tested pure fns); did not wire any frontend (out of fence, e7m.3/e7m.4).
close_reason: Verified: catalog service (LiteLLM+OpenRouter dual-source, 614 models/52 providers live-probed, 340 openrouter-runnable), 11 unit tests green incl. TTL/fallback/filtering/atomic-write, non-blocking startup warm-up, 2KB bundled snapshot, enabledProviders setting, GET catalog/models + refresh surfaces.

title: Live checkpoint: model selection end-to-end (claude + codex + OpenRouter routes)
description: Verify the whole epic in the real app with real runs. Scenarios: (1) Settings: per-purpose model dropdowns are catalog-populated and searchable; changes persist across restart; (2) provider toggles: disable a suite → its models vanish from every picker; re-enable → back; (3) catalog lifecycle: Refresh button updates freshness; simulate offline start (network blocked) → app boots instantly with cached catalog, no errors; (4) in-app ask with settings default → run uses it (verify invocation in run log); (5) per-run override to a DIFFERENT Anthropic model → override used, settings unchanged; (6) OpenAI model chosen → routes to codex CLI, completes end-to-end; (7) a third-party model (e.g. a Google or Mistral id) → routes via OpenRouter, completes a real follow-up answer; run log shows the route, never the token; (8) missing OpenRouter key → those models disabled in pickers with hint; add key in Settings → immediately pickable; run works; (9) 'Ask now' + batch 'Ask follow-ups' + Apply each honor overrides; (10) retry of a failed overridden run reuses the override and displays it; (11) invalid custom model id → legible failure surfaced in UI, retry after fixing works. Fix small issues inline; file beads for anything structural. Close-out: docs/api.md + docs/cli.md current, work-log entry, remove item 2 from docs/future-improvements.md (superseded), add the LiteLLM-proxy/local-models idea there as Phase-2.
design: Blocked by all sibling beads. Needs codex CLI authenticated AND an OpenRouter API key on this machine — if either is unavailable, checkpoint the available routes and leave a narrowly-scoped follow-up for the missing one rather than blocking the epic.
acceptance_criteria: All eleven scenarios pass live; docs and future-improvements updated; gates green (just check, cargo test --workspace, npm run build); fresh bundle installed per the install-refresh sequence.

title: Point-of-ask override UI: compact agent/model picker on every trigger
description: Add a compact, unobtrusive override picker at every point that triggers a run, defaulting to the settings-resolved value and overriding for THAT RUN ONLY: (1) NewThreadComposer (in-app ask); (2) CommentsSidebar batch 'Ask follow-ups' and 'Apply to artifact' actions; (3) per-comment/chain 'Ask now'; (4) the generation/retry path reuses the original run's override automatically (backend behavior from conceptify-e7m.1 — the retry UI should DISPLAY what it will use). Interaction: a small pill showing the effective model (e.g. 'sonnet-5' with a subtle route hint), click to open the SHARED searchable catalog combobox from conceptify-e7m.3 — grouped by provider, filtered to enabled provider suites, Custom… entry included; selection is per-invocation state, NOT persisted to settings. Models routed via OpenRouter (e7m.7) are pickable here too; if the key is missing, the picker disables them with an inline 'add key in Settings' hint rather than letting the run fail. 'Ask now' needs an especially compact affordance (it lives on a comment row) — consider the row's hover/expanded state rather than always-visible. Plumb through the override params added in conceptify-e7m.1. CLI override flags (--model on ask-triggering commands) in scope if cheap, else note in future-improvements.md.
design: Depends on conceptify-e7m.1 (override params), e7m.6 (catalog), and the shared combobox from e7m.3. Keep one reusable <ModelOverridePicker>.
acceptance_criteria: Every trigger point shows the effective model and can override it for that run from the catalog-backed picker; the spawned run demonstrably uses the override and correct route (verify via run log/DB); overrides never mutate stored settings; retry shows and reuses the original override; keyless OpenRouter models disabled with hint; UI stays visually quiet; both themes; gates green.

title: Settings UI: catalog-driven model dropdowns + provider suite toggles + OpenRouter key
description: SettingsView currently exposes adapters/models in a raw, template-oriented way. Rework around the model catalog (conceptify-e7m.6): (1) PER-PURPOSE MODEL DROPDOWNS (Follow-up answers / Artifact updates / In-app asks) populated from the catalog API, grouped by provider, searchable/filterable given the size (a plain <select> is not acceptable UX for hundreds of models — use a filterable combobox in the app's design language), plus a 'Custom…' free-text escape hatch for ids not in the catalog; (2) PROVIDER SUITE TOGGLES — a checklist of providers ('Anthropic', 'OpenAI', 'Google', 'Mistral', …, with model counts) driving the enabledProviders setting; disabled suites disappear from every dropdown including point-of-ask pickers; sensible default: anthropic + openai on; (3) each purpose row shows the RESOLVED ROUTE at a glance (e.g. 'via claude CLI', 'via codex CLI', 'via OpenRouter') per e7m.7's routing rule; (4) OPENROUTER API KEY field (masked input, stored per e7m.7's storage decision) with inline hint that it's required for non-Anthropic/OpenAI models; (5) catalog freshness row: 'Model list updated <relative time>' + a Refresh button hitting the refresh command; offline/fetch-failure shows the cached-data state quietly, never an error dialog; (6) validation inline (empty model, model from a disabled provider, missing key for an OpenRouter-routed choice); (7) advanced raw adapter-template editing stays, collapsed and visually secondary. Follow the vxc design language.
design: Depends on e7m.6 (catalog API) and e7m.7 (route display + key storage). Combobox: build a small reusable one on cfy-input primitives (no new UI dependency without checking with the orchestrator). The same combobox is reused by e7m.4 — design it as a shared component from the start.
acceptance_criteria: User can pick any catalog model per purpose via searchable grouped dropdowns; provider toggles filter all pickers app-wide; custom id entry works; route shown per purpose; OpenRouter key enterable and masked; catalog refresh button works and freshness is visible; invalid states inline-validated; both themes clean; gates green.
notes: Frontend impl decisions (medium-worker): (1) PROVIDER TOGGLES AUTO-PERSIST. get_model_catalog filters models to the SAVED enabled_providers server-side (build_response; empty-enabled => no models), so a toggle can only take effect app-wide (incl. e7m.4 point-of-ask, which reads the same saved setting) by persisting immediately. Toggling calls set_agent_settings({...lastSaved, enabledProviders}) then re-fetches the catalog. Based on lastSaved (NOT the live form) so a mid-edit model/adapter change is not force-committed and a broken Advanced adapters-JSON can't block a toggle. Main Save still persists per-purpose models + enabledProviders together. title: Codex adapter: verified built-in default (headless invocation + scoping)
description: Make codex a first-class adapter alongside claude in default_adapters() (src-tauri/src/settings.rs), not just a config-only possibility. This is investigation + implementation: (1) determine the correct codex CLI headless invocation for our contract — non-interactive exec with a prompt arg, model selection flag, machine-parseable output the run engine can consume (runs.rs parses claude stream-json; codex output likely differs — decide whether the run-progress parser needs an adapter-specific mode or whether plain stdout passthrough to the log tail is acceptable for v1, and document the choice); (2) permission scoping equivalent to OQ3 (claude gets acceptEdits + allowedTools fencing; find codex's sandbox/approval flags — e.g. its sandbox modes — and pin an equivalently-safe default: repo-writable, no network beyond what the flow needs, no mutating git); (3) working-dir + prompt template placeholders ({prompt},{model},{project_root}) wired like the claude adapter; (4) skill availability: conceptify answers rely on the conceptify skill + CLI being reachable from the agent — verify codex can follow references/follow-ups.md-style prompts or adjust the prompt templates per-adapter if needed (flows currently build one prompt shape; if codex needs different framing, that's a finding to record and scope — do NOT silently fork prompt logic); (5) doctor: extend the agent-binary check to report both claude and codex presence with install hints; absence of codex must NOT fail doctor when claude is the default; (6) verify LIVE: a real codex headless run answering a trivial follow-up end-to-end. Requires codex CLI installed and authenticated on this machine — if it is not, STOP and surface that to the orchestrator/user rather than guessing at flags from training data; verify every flag against the installed version's --help.
design: High-tier bead: real design latitude (output parsing strategy, scoping model) and security-relevant flags. The existing test at settings.rs:983 proves the adapter mechanism needs no code change — this bead is about choosing CORRECT, SAFE defaults and proving them live. If codex's stream output can't drive run-progress lines, v1 fallback = spinner + elapsed clock only (frontend already has this), with a note in future-improvements.
acceptance_criteria: default_adapters() ships claude + codex; a live codex run completes a follow-up answer end-to-end (comment answered in DB, run success); scoping flags documented in settings.rs module docs with the same rigor as the OQ3 notes; doctor reports both agents; all flag choices verified against the installed codex CLI version, not assumed; gates green.
notes: Close-reason correction (bd close applied the sibling's reason to both): e7m.2 verified as — codex built-in adapter with OQ3-grade pinned scoping (workspace-write + explicit network enable after discovering the default blocks ALL network incl. loopback; HOME kernel-denied; --ephemeral --ignore-user-config; approval implicitly never in exec). stdout-passthrough parsing (no --json v1). doctor reports both agents, codex informational. Live proof independently reproduced by orchestrator: real codex + gpt-5.4-mini answered a follow-up through the shipped template in an isolated harness, 21.7s, comment answered in DB.
close_reason: Verified: catalog service (LiteLLM+OpenRouter dual-source, 614 models/52 providers live-probed, 340 openrouter-runnable), 11 unit tests green incl. TTL/fallback/filtering/atomic-write, non-blocking startup warm-up, 2KB bundled snapshot, enabledProviders setting, GET catalog/models + refresh surfaces.

title: Backend: per-run agent/model override plumbing through the flow layer
description: Add optional per-run overrides so a single ask can use a different adapter and/or model than the configured defaults, without touching stored settings. Scope: (1) settings.rs — extend resolution so callers can pass an override {adapter?, model?} that wins over default_adapter / models.for_purpose(purpose); validate the adapter key exists (same rule as default_adapter validation) and reject empty/injection-unsafe model strings consistently with existing validation; (2) flows.rs — ask_from_app, ask_follow_ups, apply_to_artifact, ask_single_comment, and retry_ask accept an optional override param (retry should reuse the ORIGINAL run's override — decide whether to persist the override on the run row or re-pass from the frontend; persisting on runs is more robust, note the migration if chosen); (3) Tauri commands + any HTTP endpoints that front these flows get the optional field (backward compatible — omitted means current behavior); (4) expose the available adapters + configured per-purpose models to the frontend (extend the existing settings-read command/endpoint if it doesn't already return them in a UI-friendly shape); (5) tests: override wins over purpose default, unknown adapter rejected, omitted override identical to today (exact-string prompt/arg tests updated), retry reuses override. Update docs/api.md for any endpoint changes.
design: Purely additive API — keep Purpose-based defaults as the fallback chain: explicit override > per-purpose model > adapter default. NOTE (2026-07-05 rescope): with provider-based routing (conceptify-e7m.7), the user-facing choice becomes MODEL-centric — the adapter is normally DERIVED from the model's provider, so treat {model} as the primary override and keep {adapter} as an advanced escape hatch that bypasses routing. e7m.7 builds the derivation on top of this plumbing; land this first with the simple explicit form. Do NOT add per-purpose adapter config. Foundation bead: blocks the trigger-point UI bead and the routing bead.
acceptance_criteria: Flow commands accept optional {adapter, model} override; override reaches the spawned invocation's program/args verbatim (test-proven); omitted override is byte-identical to current behavior in the arg-resolution tests; retry honors the original run's override; docs updated; quality gates green.
notes:
e7m.1 IMPLEMENTED (medium-complexity-worker). Files: settings.rs (RunOverride type + resolve_with_override/selection_for/select + validate_model + InvalidModel error), runs.rs (StartRun.run_override; row records RESOLVED agent/model + persists override_json), flows.rs (all 5 flows thread the override; retry reads it back), commands.rs (4 flow commands gain run_override?; new get_agent_options), db/migrations.rs (migration 11: follow_up_runs.override_json), lib.rs (register get_agent_options), docs/api.md (Flow-commands + Settings sections only).

RETRY-PERSISTENCE DECISION: persist the override INTENT in a new nullable follow_up_runs.override_json column (plain ALTER, no rebuild), NOT just the resolved agent/model columns. Rationale: 'reuse the ORIGINAL run's override' requires that an override-FREE run re-derive CURRENT defaults on retry, while a REAL override is re-applied verbatim. Storing only resolved agent/model would freeze an override-free run to stale defaults if the user later changed per-purpose settings. retry_ask reads override_json from the most-recent run row (flows::load_latest_run_override, same ordering as latest_run_for_thread) — no frontend re-pass, robust across app restarts. A NULL/unparseable/empty blob degrades to None (current defaults). The row's agent/model columns ALSO now reflect the resolved selection (honest history) via AgentSettings::selection_for.

OTHER DECISIONS: (1) resolve() kept as a thin wrapper over resolve_with_override(purpose,root,prompt,None) so 'omitted override == byte-identical' holds by construction; existing exact-string arg tests exercise it unchanged. (2) NO HTTP endpoints front these flows (flows:: is Tauri-command-only), so only Tauri commands gained run_override; nothing added to conceptify-cli. (3) RunOverride lives in settings.rs (NOT conceptify-types) because the override never crosses the HTTP/CLI boundary — kept conceptify-cli untouched. (4) Model validation (validate_model): reject empty/whitespace-only and any embedded whitespace/control char -> SettingsError::InvalidModel; permissive on / . - : so OpenRouter/LiteLLM model ids stay valid. Structural argv safety already comes from whole-string substitution. (5) get_agent_options command exposes adapter KEYS (not full templates) + default_adapter + per-purpose models in camelCase for the e7m.4 picker; additive, get_agent_settings unchanged.

TESTS ADDED (all green, cargo test --workspace 236 passed): settings — override_model_wins, override_adapter_wins, override_adapter_and_model_together, override_unknown_adapter_rejected, override_invalid_model_rejected, omitted_and_empty_override_are_byte_identical_to_default, run_override_serde_shape_and_is_empty. runs — override_reaches_invocation_and_persists_on_row (real subprocess, argv verbatim + row cols), no_override_persists_null_and_default_selection, unknown_adapter_override_errors_before_row. flows — retry_reuses_persisted_override. migrations — add_override_json_preserves_runs_and_stores_blob. LATEST migration count 10->11. just check + clippy -D warnings clean. NOT committed/closed per orchestrator.
close_reason: Verified: RunOverride plumbing through settings/runs/flows/commands with migration 11 (override_json on run rows — retry re-derives current defaults for override-free runs, re-applies real overrides verbatim); 16 override tests + retry_reuses_persisted_override green; omitted override byte-identical by construction (resolve delegates to resolve_with_override).

title: Model selection: live catalog, provider toggles, per-ask override, provider-routed execution
description: Let the user choose which MODEL answers questions — from a live, auto-refreshing catalog — configured in Settings as per-purpose defaults and overridable at every run trigger (ask composer, Ask follow-ups, Apply, Ask now, retry). Architecture (researched 2026-07-05): (a) MODEL CATALOG — fetched on app restart from LiteLLM's model_prices_and_context_window.json (2,500+ models, provider-keyed) and/or OpenRouter's public /api/v1/models, cached on disk with a bundled offline fallback; (b) PROVIDER SUITE TOGGLES — user can enable/disable whole provider families ('only show Anthropic / OpenAI models'); (c) EXECUTION ROUTING derived from the chosen model's provider: Anthropic models → claude CLI (today's path), OpenAI models → codex CLI, ANY OTHER provider → claude CLI pointed at OpenRouter's Anthropic-compatible endpoint via env overrides (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=<OpenRouter key>, model remap) — the run engine's StartRun.env already supports per-run env, so no new agent runtime is needed; local LiteLLM proxy / self-hosted models are explicitly Phase-2. Children: e7m.1 override plumbing, e7m.2 codex adapter, e7m.6 catalog service, e7m.7 execution routing, e7m.3 settings UI, e7m.4 point-of-ask picker, e7m.5 live checkpoint.
acceptance_criteria: All child beads closed; live checkpoint passes: catalog-backed dropdowns with provider toggles, per-purpose defaults + per-run overrides, and successful end-to-end runs on all three routes (claude CLI, codex CLI, OpenRouter-via-claude).

Verify every claim against the shipped flows.rs prompts + docs (cite, don't restate). just install-skill after.

title: In-app ask: default 900s timeout is too tight for the skill's full authoring loop
description: Live QA finding (conceptify-959.5, UC5): a real in-app ask on the conceptify repo (claude-sonnet-5, question about anchor re-attachment) was killed by the 900s FR-5.3 timeout while healthy and near completion — 90 tool uses in, artifact fully assembled, running final spec checks; it had not yet reached visual self-review + save-artifact. The retry with timeoutSecs=1800 completed in ~830s (total authoring ~14min on top of the first run's burn). The skill's full loop (research + d2 renders + Shiki + agent-browser 4-frame visual review) simply costs 12-20min for a substantive artifact. Options to decide: (a) raise the default inAppAsk/artifactUpdate timeout to 1800s; (b) per-purpose timeouts (followUp can stay short); (c) skill guidance for HEADLESS runs to time-box (fewer review iterations, compact artifacts); or some combination. Note FR-5.3 itself behaved perfectly: SIGKILL of the process group, run=timeout, thread->error with working Retry.
notes: DECISION (implemented): raised the GLOBAL default timeout_secs 900 -> 1800 (30 min); did NOT add per-purpose timeouts. Rationale: the timeout is a KILL CEILING, not a fixed wait — short followUp runs still exit in seconds under a generous ceiling, so a per-purpose map would add a new settings dimension (PurposeTimeouts struct + serde defaults + frontend type + docs + backward-compat vs the existing single timeout_secs field) for near-zero benefit. 1800 is what QA validated (the run that finished in ~830s had been SIGKILLed at 900s the try before; full authoring loops cost 12-20min). FR-5.3 'configurable' preserved (single user-settable timeout_secs). Files: src-tauri/src/settings.rs (default_timeout_secs=30*60, doc comments, 2 test assertions ->1800), src/components/SettingsView.tsx (pre-load placeholder '15'->'30'), src/lib/api.ts (stale doc comment 900->1800). docs/api.md states no numeric default (no change). NOTE: prd.md still says 'default 15 min' (FR-5.3) — left untouched per file boundary; this bead is the deliberate evidence-based override of that default. Gates green: just check, cargo test --workspace (195 pass), npm run build. NOT closed/committed (orchestrator).

title: Migration: extend follow_up_runs.mode CHECK with 'ask' (in-app ask runs)
description: The shipped follow_up_runs DDL constrains mode with CHECK (mode IN ('answer','apply')), so the in-app ask flow (conceptify-959.1) cannot record its runs as mode='ask' without a schema change. Decision recorded on conceptify-b12.2: in-app asks SHOULD be follow_up_runs rows (third mode 'ask' -> Purpose::InAppAsk) so they get the same log/status/guard machinery. SQLite cannot ALTER a CHECK constraint, so this needs an appended migration that rebuilds the table (create new table with CHECK (mode IN ('answer','apply','ask')), INSERT INTO ... SELECT, drop old, rename, recreate idx_follow_up_runs_thread_id) per the append-only contract in src-tauri/src/db/migrations.rs. Engine change is then trivial: add RunMode::Ask in src-tauri/src/runs.rs (as_str 'ask', purpose InAppAsk).
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (not closed/committed per orchestrator).

MIGRATION (src-tauri/src/db/migrations.rs): appended FOLLOW_UP_RUNS_ASK_MODE as migration #9 (user_version 9). Table rebuild: CREATE follow_up_runs_new (identical DDL, only CHECK widened to mode IN ('answer','apply','ask')) -> INSERT..SELECT naming all 9 cols explicitly (started_at/finished_at copied verbatim, never re-defaulted) -> DROP old -> RENAME -> recreate idx_follow_up_runs_thread_id. status stays free-form TEXT (no CHECK), PK + REFERENCES threads(id) ON DELETE CASCADE preserved.

FOREIGN KEYS: no PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF dance. It is (a) impossible: db::open_and_migrate runs the chain with FKs ON and rusqlite_migration wraps each migration in a txn, inside which PRAGMA foreign_keys is a no-op; (b) unnecessary: follow_up_runs is a leaf (only outbound FK to threads, nothing references it), so DROP+RENAME cannot orphan/rewrite anything. Attached .foreign_key_check() to THIS M::up only (the sole rebuild in the chain) so PRAGMA foreign_key_check validates integrity before commit — idiomatic rusqlite_migration guard for a rebuild.

ENGINE (src-tauri/src/runs.rs): added RunMode::Ask -> as_str 'ask', purpose() -> Purpose::InAppAsk (Purpose::InAppAsk already existed in settings.rs). Ask carries #[allow(dead_code)] until 959.1 constructs it (same holding pattern as the active_run_for_thread wrapper). Left the #[allow(dead_code)] on active_run_for_thread in place — nothing new uses it yet (959.1 will).

TESTS: new #[cfg(test)] mod in migrations.rs — version-pinned rebuild-safety proof (to_version(8) insert answer+apply rows w/ hand-set timestamps + assert old CHECK rejects 'ask', to_version(9), assert rows survive byte-identical incl. copied started_at/finished_at, 'ask' inserts, 'bogus' rejected, FK to threads live, index recreated, full chain clean). runs.rs: run_mode_purposes_map_to_settings unit test + ask_mode_run_records_ask_row_and_completes end-to-end spawn test (proves engine INSERT mode='ask' lands against real migrated schema via db::init_at).

GATES: just check clean (clippy -D warnings). cargo test --workspace green (177 lib + 22 cli). NOTE: flows::tests::ask_follow_ups_spawns_answer_run_with_prompt_and_cli_on_path is intermittently flaky (2/3 pass in isolation) — pre-existing timestamp-tie ordering nondeterminism in flows.rs (ORDER BY comments.created_at with no tiebreaker), unrelated to this change and in a parallel agent's file boundary. Flagged for orchestrator.

title: Inline <code> overflows horizontally at narrow pane widths (no overflow-wrap)
description: Found during the M2 checkpoint (nsy.7) end-to-end pass. At reading-pane widths below ~458px the artifact overflows horizontally (document.documentElement.scrollWidth 458 > clientWidth), producing a page-level horizontal scrollbar / cut-off content. Root cause: inline <code> in prose has overflow-wrap:normal (design-system.css line ~481, selector 'p code, li code, td code, th code, figcaption code, summary code, dd code'). A single unbreakable token in the demo — 'artifact://thread/<slug>/artifact.v3.html' (42 chars, inside a step <li>) — refuses to break and sets a fixed ~458px minimum page width. Everything else scales/clips correctly: <pre> blocks scroll internally (overflow-x:auto, verified rectW 240 / scrollW 549), tables use .cfy-table-wrap (overflow-x:auto), SVGs honor max-width:100% (both rendered at 190px in a 288px column). Where it bites in-app: the default 800px window minus sidebar (w-56=224) + thread list (w-72=288) leaves a 288px reading pane, so it shows on first run. Mitigated after one resize by tauri-plugin-window-state persistence (PRD line 105). At >=460px pane the artifact is flawless (verified gorgeous at 640/900px in Chrome + WebKit, light+dark).
design: Add 'overflow-wrap: break-word;' to the inline-code selector in skill/design-system.css (~line 481). break-word only breaks a token when it would otherwise overflow, so normal-width rendering is unchanged, and it is inert on 'pre code' (white-space:pre) so block-code horizontal scroll is unaffected. IMPORTANT: skill/examples/demo-artifact.html embeds the scaffold VERBATIM as its first <style> block, so make the identical edit there too (keep them byte-in-sync) and re-run 'just install-skill' to refresh the installed snapshot. Optional/related (product call, not required here): widen the initial window in src-tauri/tauri.conf.json (currently 800x600) so the first-run reading pane isn't 288px. Boundary note: nsy.7 left design-system.css untouched because it is a closed-bead (nsy.5) deliverable and a parallel agent was active; acceptance permits filing.
acceptance_criteria: At a 288px viewport the demo artifact has document.documentElement.scrollWidth == clientWidth (no horizontal page overflow); long inline-code tokens wrap; normal/wide-width rendering is visually unchanged; skill/design-system.css and the embedded copy in skill/examples/demo-artifact.html stay identical; installed skill re-snapshotted.
close_reason: Fixed: overflow-wrap:break-word on inline code in scaffold + demo embedded copy; skill reinstalled

title: Skill: consider a post-process helper script for generated SVG (id stamping + style stripping)
description: rendering.md currently instructs the authoring agent to post-process d2/dot SVG by hand (strip prolog/styles/ids, base64-decode d2 group classes, derive+stamp data-cfy-id per spec §4.4). The recipe is deterministic, so a bundled scripts/postprocess-svg.mjs (mirroring scripts/highlight.mjs) would cut per-artifact effort and eliminate derivation mistakes. Decide during 57l.4 quality iteration whether hand-editing error rates justify shipping it.
notes: BUILT (decision pulled forward from 57l.4 per orchestrator: deterministic recipe, clearly cheap, kills derivation variance): skill/scripts/postprocess-svg.mjs — zero-dep node script mirroring highlight.mjs style. Implements rendering.md + artifact-spec §4.4 exactly: d2 (strip prolog, delete both <style> blocks, strict-base64-decode group classes with round-trip validation so real classes like 'text' can't false-positive, HTML-unescape, drop [0] / append -{n+1} for parallel edges [n>=1], stamp data-cfy-id namespaced under --fig) and dot (strip prolog/DOCTYPE/comments, remove positional id='graph0|nodeN|edgeN|clustN' attrs, entity-decode <g class='node|edge|cluster'> titles, stamp; root class='graph' skipped — the <figure> carries the fig id). Collisions get -2/-3 in document order seeded from ALL pre-existing data-cfy-ids, so re-runs and partial stamps stay stable. Deterministic + idempotent (verified: second run byte-identical on d2 flow/sequence/parallel-edge/container and dot cluster/parallel-edge outputs). stdin and --input/--write modes verified equal; async stdin read because fs.readFileSync(0) EAGAINs on macOS when the piping renderer is slow (latent footgun that also exists in highlight.mjs's fast-pipe usage). Mermaid SVG detected and rejected exit 2 per OQ5. Verification: 34 stamped ids across 4 real renders all pass the §4.2 regex, 0 dups; processed d2+dot SVGs spliced into a minimal artifact passed a scratch checker implementing spec §8 rules (W-ID-FORMAT/W-ID-DUP/W-ANCHOR-DIAGRAM/W-ANCHOR-HEADINGS/W-SRC-MALFORMED/W-SRC-ORPHAN/E-EXTERNAL-CODE positions) — checked against the spec by construction rather than the Rust validator because src-tauri was owned by a parallel agent. rendering.md now shows the script as primary path in the d2 and dot sections; the old hand-recipe is kept as 'Manual fallback / reference'; SKILL.md got a one-line pointer. Known quirk, acceptable: d2 sequence diagrams emit two groups per actor (box + lifeline span), so the second deterministically gets '<actor>-2'.
