title: Error, timeout, and retry paths (FR-5.3)
description: Failure hygiene for in-app asks (PRD FR-5.3): agent crash or timeout (default 15 min, configurable) → thread status 'error', log viewable from the thread, one-click retry (re-spawns with the same question into the same thread). Applies the N4 rule: a failed run never corrupts thread state.
acceptance_criteria: Simulated crash and timeout both land the thread in 'error' with the log accessible; retry succeeds and the thread proceeds to ready; no orphaned processes or stuck 'generating' states.
notes: Implemented together with 959.1 (see 959.1 notes for the status-machine decisions). 959.2-specific: retry_ask (flows.rs) re-spawns the same question into the same thread, sets 'generating' BEFORE start (closes the finish-vs-set race) and reverts to 'error' on start failure; a fresh follow_up_runs row is created each retry (failed run's log stays on disk). Crash (exit!=0), timeout (settings.timeout_secs, default 900s), and cancel all land the thread in 'error' via the shared watcher's conditional generating->error. Log is viewable from the thread-view error panel via get_latest_run -> get_run_log_tail (works after restart; N4 boot reconciliation marks a stale 'running' row 'failed'). No orphaned processes: reuses runs.rs process-group SIGKILL on cancel/timeout. Flow tests cover crash->error, timeout->error, completed-without-artifact->error, save-then-complete stays ready (no regress), and retry->generating->ready.

title: OQ3: permission scoping for headless runs
description: Open question 3 (PRD §12): headless runs currently use --permission-mode acceptEdits — scoped how? Artifact-apply runs only need to write within the thread folder. Investigate per-adapter sandboxing/allowed-tools flags (Claude Code allowed-tools, permission modes; whatever Codex offers later) during M5 and pick the tightest workable scoping. Right-sized security (§9): containment and hygiene, not adversarial defense.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded; adapter default args updated to the chosen scoping; documented in the skill/README so future adapters follow the same principle.
notes:
OQ3 DECIDED (verified empirically against installed claude CLI 2.1.201 with three tiny headless probes, claude-haiku-4-5):

DECISION — default claude adapter args are now:
-p {prompt} --model {model} --permission-mode acceptEdits --output-format stream-json --strict-mcp-config --allowedTools Bash Edit Write --disallowedTools WebFetch WebSearch Bash(git commit:*) Bash(git push:*) Bash(git add:*) Bash(git rebase:*) Bash(git merge:*) Bash(git reset:*) Bash(git checkout:*) Bash(git switch:*) Bash(git restore:*) Bash(git stash:*) Bash(git clean:*) Edit(/{project_root}/**) Write(/{project_root}/**)

KEY FINDING: the previous PRD-default template (acceptEdits alone) was BROKEN for real headless runs — in -p mode acceptEdits auto-approves only safelisted read-only Bash (probe: echo OK, mktemp -d DENIED 'command requires approval'), so every conceptify CLI call / renderer would have been denied. --allowedTools Bash Edit Write is load-bearing, not optional.

PROBE EVIDENCE: (1) acceptEdits alone: Bash echo OK, Write in cwd OK, mktemp DENIED. (2) with allow+deny set: mktemp OK, Write out-of-cwd OK, Write into denied abs dir DENIED, git log OK, git commit DENIED via Bash(git commit:*), WebSearch removed from toolset entirely. (3) canonical Write(//abs/**) deny form (what Edit(/{project_root}/**) resolves to, since project_root starts with /) DENIED. Denies beat allows+acceptEdits. Patterns passed as one argv element each (Bash(git commit:*) contains a space; comma-joined single arg riskier).

SCOPING PRINCIPLE (all modes, incl. future InAppAsk + future adapters): target repo READ-ONLY (Edit/Write denied under project_root; git reads allowed, mutating git denied); temp dirs writable; arbitrary local Bash allowed (CLI contract + d2/dot/node); no web tools; no user MCP servers (--strict-mcp-config).

REJECTED: bypassPermissions (zero containment for zero gain); fine-grained Bash whitelist e.g. Bash(conceptify:*) (prefix rules break on env-assignments/pipes/quoting; each headless denial is a wasted flail — the prison-that-breaks-the-product §9 warns about); --tools whitelist (brittle enumeration of built-ins across CLI versions; disallow already removes a tool from the toolset); PER-PURPOSE arg overrides (e.g. deny Bash(conceptify save-artifact:*) in answer runs) — all three purposes need the same shape, would add an adapter data-model dimension for an accident that is prompt-forbidden and versioning-recoverable; revisit only if answer runs are observed publishing versions; sandbox-exec / network firewalling (adversarial-grade, §9 out of scope). NOT addressed (noted, deliberate): target repo's own .claude project settings still load in -p mode (CLI denies still win over any allow there); Bash file writes into the repo (sed -i etc.) not blockable at rule level — acceptable accident hygiene tradeoff.

IMPLEMENTED: settings.rs default_adapters() + full rationale in doc comment + new default_claude_scoping_exact pin test (29 args) + adversarial test updated; flows.rs: one toolset-scope line added to both prompts' Hard rules (agent told constraints so it doesn't flail) + both exact-string tests updated in lockstep; docs/api.md: 'Permission scoping' section after Child environment. FOLLOW-UP for skill docs (skill/ untouchable this session, another agent active): skill/README or references should echo the future-adapters principle — repo read-only, temp writable, no web, no mutating git.

title: OQ4: confirm early thread creation (generating status) feels right
description: Open question 4 (PRD §12): current spec creates the thread BEFORE the artifact exists (UC1 shows a 'generating' thread early — better UX) with error status + retry on agent failure, vs. only registering threads on save-artifact (no orphan threads). Confirm the chosen behavior feels right during M3 real-repo iteration; if orphaned error-threads become noise, revisit. Record the confirmation or the change.
acceptance_criteria: Decision confirmed or reversed based on M3 experience; thread-creation flow and skill updated if changed.
notes: OQ4 VERDICT (confirmed, 2026-07-04 M3 iteration): keep create-thread-early. Across all M3 runs (UC1/UC2/UC3 + the real-HOME meta run) the thread appearing instantly with a pulsing 'generating' chip and progress placeholder is clearly the right UX — the app feels alive during the 10-30 min authoring window, and the CLI flow (ensure-project -> create-thread -> author -> save-artifact) reads naturally. The generating->ready flip inside the save transaction was observed working every time (thread visible as generating during authoring, ready+focused on save). The one genuine cost, verified by deliberately abandoning a run mid-authoring: an abandoned thread stays 'generating' forever with NO delete affordance and no staleness signal — an immortal pulsing chip. That is noise, but rare (1 orphan across ~10 M3 runs) and does not outweigh the UX win; it needs a hygiene valve, not a redesign. Filed conceptify-0kt (P3, discovered-from this bead) for delete/expire affordance. No change to thread-creation flow or skill.

title: OQ5: is Mermaid pre-rendering worth its weight in the skill?
description: Open question 5 (PRD §12): decide empirically during M3 iteration whether Mermaid pre-rendering (mermaid-cli/Puppeteer toolchain) earns its place in the skill, or whether D2 + Graphviz DOT + hand-authored SVG covers all structured-diagram needs (§8 Tier 1). Weigh: toolchain weight and flakiness vs. cases where Mermaid's syntax reliability wins. Record the decision and update the skill's visual-strategy section accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded with evidence from M3 iterations; skill guide updated to match (either exact mermaid render commands, or explicit 'use D2/DOT instead' guidance).
notes: OQ5 DECIDED (empirically, 2026-07-04): Mermaid pre-rendering is NOT in the skill toolchain; D2 + Graphviz DOT + hand-authored SVG stay the complete Tier-1 set. Evidence (mmdc 11.16.0 via npx, this machine): (1) WEIGHT — first npx run ~70s and downloaded full Chrome + chrome-headless-shell (~550MB) into ~/.cache/puppeteer; every render boots headless Chrome (~3.5s/diagram vs ~0.3s for d2; d2/dot need no network ever). (2) THEMING FRAGILITY — themeVariables are silently ignored unless theme:'base' (verified: sentinels landed 0x with theme:'neutral', correctly with 'base'); neo/handDrawn sequence actors bake literal #eaeaea/#666 from variables outside the D5 token table (actorBkg etc.), so staying on-system needs per-diagram-type variable maps; handDrawn roughjs paths 4x the file size (16KB->64KB for a 5-node flowchart). (3) ANCHORABILITY (decisive) — state-diagram edges emit only positional ids (edge0, edge1) which artifact-spec §4.2 forbids; sequence messages have no bounding <g> grouping line+label; every mmdc SVG is id='my-svg' with #my-svg-scoped <style> and url(#my-svg-*) markers, colliding when an artifact has 2+ diagrams; flowchart/state labels are HTML-in-foreignObject. Flowchart node/edge ids ARE semantic (flowchart-client-0, L_client_middleware_0), but flowcharts are exactly where d2 already wins. (4) WHAT WE FORGO — denser sequence diagrams (notes/activations) and gantt/ER/gitgraph/journey. Mitigations: d2 sequence output is clean (labels knocked out via SVG mask — apparent overlap in rsvg thumbnails is a converter artifact, browsers render correctly); gantt/ER in an explanation artifact are usually better as cfy-table/cfy-steps; genuinely interactive cases keep Tier-2 runtime mermaid (pinned CDN + pre fallback), unchanged. REVISIT IF: 57l.4 shows d2 sequence quality failing on real content; artifacts repeatedly need gantt/ER at generation time; or a browser-free mermaid renderer ships. rendering.md updated with a 'Mermaid pre-rendering — NOT in the toolchain (OQ5)' section; scripts/postprocess-svg.mjs rejects mermaid SVG with a pointer to it. ELK note for the record: layout:'elk' does work in mmdc (verified vs dagre), irrelevant given the decision.

title: conceptify doctor: prerequisites check (FR-6.5)
description: 'conceptify doctor' CLI command (PRD FR-6.5): checks app installed, CLI on PATH, d2 present, graphviz (dot) present, node present (for Shiki), agent binary resolvable (login-shell lookup per §5.1); prints actionable per-check pass/fail with install hints. Skill docs reference it as the first debugging step; prerequisites also documented in the repo.
acceptance_criteria: Doctor reports each dependency with clear pass/fail and remediation hint; exits non-zero when anything required is missing; documented in README/skill.

title: Skill self-review loop (FR-6.3)
description: De-risk the highest-variance tier — hand-authored SVG (PRD FR-6.3): before save-artifact, the skill instructs the agent to render the HTML (headless browser/screenshot) and review for overlapping labels, broken layout, and contrast issues, iterating until clean. Define the exact mechanism (which headless renderer, what the review checklist is) and bake it into the skill.
acceptance_criteria: Skill includes a working, copy-pasteable self-review procedure; demonstrated catching at least one real layout defect (e.g. overlapping SVG labels) before publish during M3 iteration.
notes:
FR-6.3 visual self-review loop (57l.2) — mechanism + verification:

MECHANISM: agent-browser (bundled headless Chromium, CDP) — the proven path from nsy.5/57l.1. Recipe: open file://<artifact>, then for each of 2 widths x 2 modes: 'set viewport <w> 900 2' + 'set media light|dark' + 'screenshot --full <png>', then Read the PNGs and judge against a checklist. Widths 460 (narrow thread-pane / overflow-risk) and 900 (comfortable); deviceScaleFactor 2 for crisp text. set viewport/set media apply live to the next screenshot — no reload needed (verified). Rejected alternatives: app-window screencapture (TCC-blocked, per prior beads), Playwright-via-npx (not installed here — confirmed unreliable; kept only as a best-effort fallback note).

MECHANICAL OVERFLOW TRIPWIRE (novel, verified): a '--full' PNG's pixelWidth == viewport_width x scale EXACTLY when there is no horizontal overflow (920 at 460/scale2; 1800 at 900/scale2). Anything wider = horizontal overflow at that width. 'sips -g pixelWidth' gives a cheap deterministic narrow-pane-overflow detector to pair with the visual read.

ANIMATION NOTE: headless Chromium runs animations to completion, so it CANNOT reproduce the WKWebView occlusion-suspension from-state trap — that stays a SOURCE check (scaffold ban, restated in checklist). Documented explicitly.

DELIVERABLES: new skill/references/self-review.md (recipe + mechanical check + 8-item checklist + loop + fallback); SKILL.md step 5 rewritten from placeholder to Source review + Visual review (points at self-review.md), and self-review.md added to the 'Files in this skill' list. install-skill picks it up automatically (cp -R skill/.) — no justfile change. Reinstalled; verified present in ~/.claude/skills/conceptify/.

VERIFIED E2E: golden fixture skill/examples/demo-artifact.html rendered clean on all 4 frames (widths match exactly: 920/920/1800/1800). Sabotaged scratchpad copy with 3 injected defects all caught: (1) overlapping SVG labels (moved fig-serve-path handler label x 350->150) visible in every frame; (2) hardcoded #211c15 fill on the hand-sketch 'opaque-origin iframe' label — readable in light, INVISIBLE in dark (dark-mode contrast break); (3) white-space:nowrap long command -> narrow-frame pixelWidth 1594 (>920) = horizontal overflow at 460px, exactly 1800 (clean) at 900px. Screenshots under scratchpad/57l2/.

title: justfile: dev / build / install-cli
description: Developer entry points (PRD §11 M0): a justfile with at minimum 'just dev' (app dev loop), 'just build' (release build of app + CLI), and 'just install-cli' (build the CLI and symlink it into /usr/local/bin or ~/.local/bin so it's on PATH for agents). Keep it the single place build/install knowledge lives — the M6 setup script and README will call into it.
acceptance_criteria: just dev / just build / just install-cli each work from a clean checkout; after install-cli, 'conceptify status' resolves from a fresh shell.
close_reason: justfile with dev/build/install-cli (+check) verified from clean state; install-cli symlinks to ~/.local/bin, fresh-shell 'conceptify status' works

title: App lifecycle: single-instance, hide-on-close, window-state
description: Lifecycle behavior (PRD §5.1): tauri-plugin-single-instance as duplicate-launch guard; window close HIDES instead of quitting (CloseRequested + prevent_close) so the HTTP API stays up while no window is visible; explicit Quit from the app menu; tauri-plugin-window-state for size/position persistence. This is what makes the CLI liveness contract cheap — the app being 'running' doesn't require a visible window.
design: PRD §5.1 Lifecycle. Menu-bar/tray mode is Phase 2 — plain hidden window is enough for Phase 1.
acceptance_criteria: Launching the app twice focuses the existing instance instead of starting a second; closing the window leaves the process alive and /health responding; reopening (e.g. via open -a) restores the window at its previous size/position; menu Quit actually exits.
close_reason: single-instance (registered first), hide-on-close via CloseRequested+prevent_close, macOS Reopen restore, window-state plugin; default menu Quit exits (ExitRequested not intercepted); verified live where headless allows

title: Codex adapter refinements: per-adapter prompt scope note + structured progress parsing
description: Two refinements deferred from conceptify-e7m.2 (codex adapter v1). (1) PROMPT SCOPE NOTE: the flow prompts' 'Your toolset is scoped: web tools are disabled, git commands that mutate the repo are denied, and your Edit/Write tools cannot touch files inside the project root' sentence describes the claude adapter's tool rules. Under the codex adapter the behavioral rules still hold as instructions, but the mechanism description is partly inaccurate: codex's workspace-write sandbox makes the repo (incl. .git) writable and has no per-command git deny (verified live on codex-cli 0.142.0: git commit succeeds in-sandbox), while $HOME and everything outside repo+/tmp+$TMPDIR is kernel-denied. Consider a per-adapter scope-note substitution in the prompt builders (do NOT fork whole prompts). (2) STRUCTURED PROGRESS: codex exec emits its transcript on stderr and only the final message on stdout; v1 ships plain passthrough (run-progress degrades to kind 'output', frontend shows elapsed clock + log tail). When codex's --json JSONL event schema stabilizes, evaluate an adapter-aware parse mode in runs.rs (claude stream-json vs codex JSONL) so codex runs get real progress kinds. See settings.rs default_adapters() docs and bead conceptify-e7m.2 notes for the verified 0.142.0 behavior.
notes:
Part 1 IMPLEMENTED (per-adapter prompt scope note). Part 2 (structured codex progress) PARKED in docs/future-improvements.md #5. Bead left in_progress (orchestrator closes).

DESIGN CHOICE: (b) template placeholder substituted in runs after routing — NOT (a). Rationale: routing::route_run stays the SINGLE call, inside runs::start_reserved exactly where the architecture puts it (routing.rs/runs.rs module docs: routing runs right before resolution so every failure surfaces before any run row exists and never wedges the FR-4.9 guard). The flow builders emit a sentinel (flows::SCOPE_MECHANISM_PLACEHOLDER, U+2063-fenced so it cannot collide with reader-supplied prompt text and carries no braces/NUL); after route_run decides the adapter, start_reserved calls flows::apply_scope_note(&req.prompt, &route.adapter) and feeds the result to resolve_with_override + the log char count. The scope note is thus filled from the SAME decision that drives execution => prompt text and actual route cannot diverge, and routing is never computed twice. Option (a) would either compute the route twice (divergence risk) or move routing out of start_reserved (reopens a settled architectural decision, plumbs secret-bearing RouteDecision.env through StartRun) — rejected.

Only the MECHANISM clause is per-adapter; behavioral rules identical (no web research, no mutating git, never edit target repo; write only to scratch/temp). Per-adapter clauses (flows::scope_mechanism):
- claude: 'Your toolset is scoped: web tools are disabled, git commands that mutate the repo are denied, and your Edit/Write tools cannot touch files inside the project root' (BYTE-IDENTICAL to pre-w9e prompt)
- codex: 'Your toolset runs in a workspace-write sandbox: the kernel lets you write inside the project directory, /tmp, and $TMPDIR and denies everything outside them (including $HOME). It does not block web access, mutating git, or writes inside the project itself, so keep these as firm rules regardless: do no web research, run no git commands that mutate the repo, and do not edit the target repo' (matches verified codex-cli 0.142.0 posture in settings.rs default_adapters())
- fallback (any other/custom adapter, routed manual): 'Keep your work within these rules whatever your tools allow: do no web research, run no git commands that mutate the repo, and do not edit files inside the project root' (instruction-only, no mechanism claim)
Line assembled as: '- {mechanism} — read the project freely, but write only under {write_location}.' where write_location stays per-flow (scratch directory / working directory copy / temp working directory).

FILES: src-tauri/src/flows.rs (sentinel const + scope_mechanism + apply_scope_note; 3 builders emit sentinel; doc updated), src-tauri/src/runs.rs (apply_scope_note call after routing). routing.rs untouched (no plumbing needed). docs/api.md untouched (it summarizes 'toolset is scoped', does not quote the sentence — still true for both adapters).

TESTS: 4 existing exact-string prompt tests routed through apply_scope_note('claude') (expected strings unchanged, proving claude parity); added answer_prompt_exact_codex (full codex prompt) and scope_note_lines_exact_for_every_adapter_and_flow (exact scope line for all 3 write-locations x claude/codex/fallback, asserts sentinel never survives); extended ask_follow_ups_spawns... to assert the sentinel is substituted before spawn and the fallback note reaches the fake adapter (custom default => manual route). just check + cargo test --workspace green (274 + 24 pass).
close_reason: Part 1 implemented: per-adapter scope-note substitution via sentinel placeholder filled in runs after route_run (single source of truth, no route recomputation); claude note byte-identical to pre-w9e (pin tests unchanged), codex note matches verified 0.142.0 sandbox posture, instruction-only fallback for custom adapters; exact-string tests per adapter×flow + end-to-end spawn assertion. Part 2 (structured codex progress) parked in docs/future-improvements.md §5 pending codex --json schema stability.

title: Tests can leak proj-* artifact dirs into the real ~/Documents/conceptify/artifacts
description: Observed during the M5/M6 live checkpoint: ~16 empty 'proj-cancel-*/proj-fail-*/proj-guard-*/proj-nospawn-*/proj-ok-*/proj-ask-*' dirs (each containing threads/run-test/runs/<uuid>.log) accumulated in the REAL artifacts root, timestamped to earlier cargo test runs. The CONCEPTIFY_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR escape hatch in artifacts::artifacts_root() is set lazily by shared_artifacts_root() in runs.rs/flows.rs tests; any code path that resolves artifacts_root() before a test has set the var (test-ordering race across the process) falls through to the real ~/Documents root. Fix: set the env var eagerly (ctor/once at test-binary start) or thread an explicit root through the test harness instead of process-global lazy env. Cleanup: the leaked dirs under ~/Documents/conceptify/artifacts/proj-* are safe to delete manually (none correspond to DB project ids).
notes:
ROOT CAUSE: artifacts::artifacts_root() had a #[cfg(test)] env-var escape hatch that FELL THROUGH to real ~/Documents when CONCEPTIFY_TEST_ARTIFACTS_DIR was unset. Production code (runs::execute_run at runs.rs:539, commands.rs, artifact_protocol.rs, artifacts_routes.rs) calls artifacts_root() DURING a test body; the env var was only set LAZILY by each module's shared_artifacts_root() (called first from harness Drop, not setup), so the first test to hit artifacts_root() before any harness set the var wrote proj-*/threads/run-test/runs/<uuid>.log into real Documents — and Drop then cleaned the (empty) temp subtree, leaving the real dirs. Parallel #[tokio::test] widened the race.

STRUCTURAL FIX (cannot recur): gated the real dirs::document_dir() branch of artifacts_root() behind #[cfg(not(test))] and made the #[cfg(test)] branch resolve ONLY to a per-process temp scratch root via new pub(crate) artifacts::test_artifacts_root() (OnceLock -> at most one set_var/process; honors an explicitly pinned env override else derives temp_dir()/conceptify-test-artifact-roots-<pid>). The real path is now UNREACHABLE in test builds — no fall-through exists. Centralized the 3 duplicated harness helpers (runs.rs, flows.rs, server/artifacts_routes.rs shared_artifacts_root) to delegate to test_artifacts_root(), removing their multi-writer env races. Added regression test artifacts::tests::artifacts_root_never_resolves_under_real_documents. Chose auto-scratch over the 'panic when unset' option because it's a stronger guarantee (path physically absent from test binary) and avoids ordering-dependent panics in the parallel suite.

VERIFY: ls diff of ~/Documents/conceptify/artifacts before vs after full cargo test --workspace = NO CHANGE (0 new dirs); all writes landed under /var/folders/.../T/conceptify-test-artifact-roots-<pid>. Gates green: just check (clippy -D warnings), cargo test --workspace (195 pass incl new regression), npm run build.

CLEANUP: deleted 15 leaked test dirs (all proj-*, none a DB project id): proj-ask-9249-*, proj-cancel-{86208,86598,86826,87442,87578,88575}-*, proj-fail-{86598,87578,88575}-*, proj-guard-{86598,87578}-*, proj-nospawn-{86598,87578}-*, proj-ok-9249-*. KEPT the two real DB-project UUID dirs 73149e94-... and 9e7f8695-.... DB ids checked: 73149e94-15c9-4a60-b8dd-00f24f26f6f7, 9e7f8695-0815-4b23-acd1-8d492faebeec, eb137dca-430b-458d-a06b-1a07049d7116. NOT closed/committed (orchestrator).

Follow-up (out of scope, not filed): harness Drop leaves empty conceptify-test-artifact-roots-<pid> dirs in the OS temp dir; harmless (self-cleaning, never Documents).

title: Thread lifecycle hygiene: delete or expire orphaned 'generating' threads
description: M3 iteration evidence (OQ4, conceptify-57l.6): when an authoring agent dies or abandons a run after create-thread, the thread sits in 'generating' forever — pulsing chip, progress placeholder, no delete affordance anywhere in the UI (observed with thread b238368b 'Abandoned: how does Router::merge work' in the isolated M3 test HOME; also plausible in real use since agents do get killed mid-run). Create-thread-early itself is confirmed as the right UX (see 57l.6 verdict) — this bead is only the hygiene valve: give the user a way to remove or retire dead generating threads. Options (pick smallest that works): (a) context-menu Delete thread (cascades comments/artifacts via existing FKs; also removes the artifacts dir); (b) auto-flag: a thread still 'generating' with no artifact after N hours renders its chip as stale/error with a retry/delete affordance. PRD §12 OQ4 anticipated exactly this: 'if orphaned error-threads become noise, revisit.'
notes: Implemented. SCOPE DECISION: general thread delete (confirm-guarded, inline on the selected thread in ThreadList), chosen over generating/error-only because the backend cascade is clean (verified). CASCADE VERIFIED (NO schema change): db::open_and_migrate sets PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON; threads->artifacts, threads->comments (both the thread_id FK and the composite artifacts FK), threads->follow_up_runs all ON DELETE CASCADE. So DELETE FROM threads cascades everything. Test lib.rs delete_thread_cascades_against_real_migrations (seeds project>thread>artifact>comment>run, deletes, asserts children gone + sibling thread/artifact untouched). Backend: threads::delete_thread(conn,id)->bool (row only, cascade) + commands::delete_thread (get_thread_opt for project/slug BEFORE delete -> delete -> best-effort remove_dir_all of artifacts/<project>/threads/<slug>, NotFound tolerated, made artifacts::thread_dir pub as single source of truth). EVENT: emits NONE - matches the codebase command convention (commands.rs header: shell refetches after awaiting; events are for cross-surface HTTP/CLI mutations, and no CLI/API delete exists). appStore.deleteThread clears selection if selected then refetches threads+projects. (b)-lite STALLED CHIP: StatusChip gained stalled prop; ThreadList marks a generating thread idle >30min (updated_at, not bumped until save) as a muted 'Stalled' chip (visual only, re-evaluated per render, no timer - 'cheap' per bead). EDGE (reasoned, not handled specially): deleting a thread with a genuinely-live run - rare for orphans - leaves the in-memory RunRegistry entry to fail its next save-artifact/resolve-comment gracefully (thread FK gone); acceptable for a dead-thread hygiene valve.
